---
name: build-workflow-skill
description: Use when an implementation-ready workflow task requires creating or materially updating a canonical Foundry skill, project-packaged skill, standalone product skill, or independently callable workflow-pack member.
---

# Build Workflow Skill

## Overview

Build the approved callable skill deliverable. A skill is a substantive
`SKILL.md` plus matching `agents/openai.yaml`; code, commands, helpers, apps, or
references cannot substitute for it. Leave tracker writes, phase transitions,
and publishing with the invoking lifecycle task.

Use GPT-5.6 Sol for every model-run evaluation. If it is unavailable, stop with
`eval-runner-unavailable`; never select a fallback model.

## Required Context

Read root and project policy, project state, every non-done task, the selected
task, previous workflow skill guidance, and the exact product repository policy when
the target is standalone. Then read:

- [the complete bundle contract](references/skill-bundle-contract.md) for
  identity, semantics, ownership variants, optional resources, and completion;
- [the behavior evaluation contract](references/behavior-evaluation-contract.md)
  for RED/GREEN isolation, evidence fields, stable failures, and release gates.

## Input Contract

Require one selected task at `removed_lifecycle_field: "implement"` with approved version-1
deliverable migration, an exact create/update operation, ownership, target
surface, visibility, runtime targets, required artifacts, guidance, evals,
completion conditions, artifact bindings, and deliverable/role/artifact-bound
phase approvals. Require a complete dependency write plan for every dependency
step.

For `update`, resolve exactly one existing skill identity. Stop on zero or
multiple matches. Never invent a new slug as an update shortcut.

## Workflow

1. Ask `native Codex planning` for the selected task and projected blockers. Do not
   repair tracker state here.
2. Match the contract to exactly one ownership branch: canonical Foundry,
   project-packaged, standalone product, or workflow-pack member.
   In a mixed task, select only contracts whose kind is a skill. A UI, package,
   validator, document, or tracker contract remains with its own builder even
   when its paths are separately approved.
3. Load the shared scenario contract. Confirm it separates lifecycle-intent
   routing from implementation-ready construction cases.
4. Capture a fresh first-attempt RED run without this builder. Keep the exact
   prompt, session, output, diff, and validation result.
5. Implement the smallest complete bundle permitted by the approved paths.
6. Run every required scenario variant independently with GPT-5.6 Sol in a
   clean disposable repository. Give the runner only the raw prompt and
   implementation-ready repository context—not the expected route or rubric.
7. Grade emitted contracts, file tree, contents, diff, ownership, visibility,
   catalog state, phase result, and dependency outcome. A narrative claim is
   not evidence.
8. Run deterministic bundle, routing, boundary, catalog, dependency, and
   command/helper regressions. Re-run the affected family after each fix.
9. Return evidence and readiness to the lifecycle task. Never mark it done.

## Decision Points

| Approved branch | Target | Visibility | Foundry catalog |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Canonical | `.agents/skills/<slug>/` | `canonical-active` | Include when complete |
| Project packaged | `projects/<project>/skills/<slug>/` | `project-local-inactive` | Exclude |
| Standalone | Exact product-owned surface | `standalone-only` | Exclude |
| Unresolved | None | unresolved | Stop with zero writes |

Each workflow-pack member needs its own skill contract, identity, complete
bundle, and invocation. Shared files remain declared support. Promotion from a
packaged or standalone surface requires a separate contract.

Never place `SKILL.md` or `agents/openai.yaml` under a non-skill target to make
that contract look complete. In particular, do not write a skill bundle into a
UI application path; return that independent deliverable as pending.

Add optional references, scripts, or assets only when the contract declares
the exact support artifact, `SKILL.md` directly consumes it, policy permits it,
and verification covers it. Keep operator invocation as `$skill-name`.
Commands are secondary and allowed only under `Developer Verification`,
`Package Smoke Test`, `Deterministic Validation`, `Query Helper`, or `Internal
Support`.

## Failure Handling

Stop with zero new writes for pending migration; missing or contradictory
contracts; unresolved ownership; unbound approvals; ambiguous update identity;
undeclared resources; unsafe dependency or external writes; incomplete,
helper-only, thin-wrapper, command-first, or code-only bundles; unavailable
GPT-5.6 Sol; or failed deterministic, fresh-agent, security, or review gates.

Return the stable code, observed evidence, and recovery owner. Do not weaken a
contract or rewrite a failed first attempt into a pass.

## Human Boundaries

The operator approves contracts, product ownership, phase paths, external
writes, promotion, publishing, and consequential dependency actions. This
skill may write only exact task-approved paths inside the declared boundary.

## Output Contract

Return deliverable identity and ownership; primary and justified support files;
catalog result; per-scenario GPT-5.6 Sol session evidence; deterministic results;
dependency provenance; validation and review; blockers; and the lifecycle task
that owns the handoff.

## Completion Gate

Apply every check in the linked bundle and behavior contracts. Completion
requires substantive semantics, exact identities, boundary-safe artifacts,
first-attempt per-variant evidence, deterministic verification, dependency
reconciliation when present, and a Standards-plus-Spec review with no critical
or high findings.

## Developer Verification

```bash
node scripts/validate-workflow-state.mjs
node scripts/query-workflow-state.mjs --skill-catalog
```
FILE:.agents/skills/build-workflow-skill/references/skill-bundle-contract.md
# Skill Bundle Contract

Use this contract for every target skill emitted by `$build-workflow-skill`.

## Identity

- Use a lowercase, hyphenated folder.
- Set frontmatter `name` to that exact folder name.
- Put trigger language in frontmatter `description`.
- Include `agents/openai.yaml` with `display_name`, `short_description`, and a
  `default_prompt` that invokes the exact `$skill-name`.
- Make target surface, ownership, runtime visibility, and runtime targets agree
  with the approved deliverable contract.

## Required Semantics

Every substantive `SKILL.md` defines:

1. Overview: the user-visible job and non-goals.
2. Required Context: exact policy, task, source, and prior-art inputs.
3. Input Contract: required and optional inputs plus invalid combinations.
4. Workflow: imperative, independently inspectable steps.
5. Decision Points: branches, selection criteria, and default-deny behavior.
6. Failure Handling: stable blockers, recovery, and stop behavior.
7. Human Boundaries: consequential actions and approvals retained by the user
   or harness.
8. Output Contract: files, state, evidence, and handoff shape.
9. Completion Gate: deterministic, model-run, human-review, and security gates.

Do not replace these semantics with a pointer to another skill or command.

## Optional Resources

Create a reference only for detailed knowledge loaded conditionally. Create a
script only for deterministic repeated work permitted by the repository file
policy. Create an asset only when the workflow directly consumes a stable
template or media resource.

For every resource, record its declared support artifact id, purpose, exact
approval, direct `SKILL.md` reference, consumer deliverable, and verification
evidence. Delete speculative or unreferenced resources.

## Ownership Variants

- Canonical: `.agents/skills/<slug>`; active in the Foundry catalog.
- Project packaged: `projects/<project-slug>/skills/<slug>`; shipped with that
  project and inactive in the Foundry runtime.
- Standalone: product-policy-owned path; inactive in the Foundry catalog and
  evaluated in a disposable repository by default.
- Workflow pack: one complete bundle and independent invocation per member.

An update resolves exactly one existing identity. Promotion is a separate
contract, not an ownership shortcut.

Apply the same strict bundle parser and semantic checks to canonical,
project-packaged, and standalone bundles. Ownership changes path and policy; it
does not permit malformed YAML, duplicate metadata keys, command-first usage,
thin wrappers, undeclared helpers, or missing required semantics.

## Completion Gate

Complete only when:

- required files exist and identities agree;
- the workflow semantics above are substantive;
- optional resources are declared, referenced, and tested;
- command-first and helper-substitute checks pass;
- runtime visibility and catalog membership are correct;
- deterministic fixtures pass;
- required GPT-5.6 Sol fresh-agent scenarios pass on first attempt;
- dependency outcomes reconcile when present;
- Standards and Spec review has no unresolved critical or high finding.
FILE:.agents/skills/build-workflow-skill/references/behavior-evaluation-contract.md
# Behavior Evaluation Contract

Use one versioned scenario source for deterministic fixtures and fresh-agent
runs. Store raw prompts, expected contracts, and observed evidence separately;
never reveal grader expectations to the fresh agent.

## Scenario Fields

Each scenario or expanded family variant carries:

- scenario id and family;
- contract version and prompt variant;
- runner modes;
- unaltered raw prompt;
- disposable repository context and policy files;
- expected intent, builder, branch, and deliverable kind;
- ownership boundary, target surface, visibility, and runtime targets;
- required primary bundle and semantics;
- allowed support;
- forbidden outcomes;
- dependency behavior and protected paths;
- starting phase, approval behavior, and expected phase result;
- expected final status, catalog membership, and duplicate count.

## RED And GREEN

1. Keep lifecycle-intent routing separate from implementation-ready
   construction. Lifecycle cases classify or stop; construction cases receive
   the already approved contract but never the hidden expected route.
2. Run a construction prompt without the target builder. Archive the output,
   zero-write proof, and exact rationalization as RED.
3. Run the same prompt with the builder in a clean disposable worktree as
   GREEN.
4. Use GPT-5.6 Sol only. If it is unavailable, record a blocked run; never use a
   fallback model.
5. Give the runner no expected route, hidden classification, forbidden outcome,
   or rubric.
6. Grade actual contract state, emitted files, diff, catalog projection,
   dependency evidence, and phase result.
7. Preserve first-attempt results and isolate every scenario from prior output.

## Evidence Fields

Record:

- evidence id, scenario id, scenario and builder contract versions;
- runner mode, explicit GPT-5.6 Sol identity, Codex CLI version, session id,
  start/completion timestamps, and `first_attempt: true`;
- raw prompt and repository context;
- exact raw runner output plus expected and observed route;
- observed ownership, deliverable contracts, artifacts, visibility, catalog
  membership, phase result, and dependency outcomes;
- full created/updated diff, or explicit verified zero-write evidence for a
  blocked/refused case;
- validation results from an independent deterministic, agent, human, or E2E
  grader with a different identity and session from the runner;
- observed rationalizations;
- pass/fail result, blocker or failure codes, and notes.

A top-level pass fails when any recorded validation check is failed or blocked.
Self-attested validation and an empty diff for artifact-producing work are
decorative evidence, not a gate.

## Failure Codes

Use stable codes:

```text
migration-pending
contract-missing
contract-invalid
ownership-unresolved
phase-approval-unbound
required-artifact-missing
required-artifact-invalid
support-artifact-undeclared
eval-evidence-missing
eval-evidence-failed
dependency-write-plan-missing
dependency-output-missing
dependency-provenance-missing
dependency-role-mismatch
dependency-out-of-boundary
catalog-bundle-incomplete
catalog-phantom-entry
review-evidence-missing
primary-deliverable-incomplete
python-substitute
javascript-only
helper-only
thin-wrapper
command-first
incomplete-metadata
identity-mismatch
decorative-eval
unreferenced-helper
```

## Release Threshold

- Every deterministic fixture passes.
- Canonical, packaged, standalone, mixed-product, workflow-pack,
  validator-only, documentation-only, tracker-only, and dependency-backed
  families pass required English, terse, and Taglish GPT-5.6 Sol runs.
- Ambiguous ownership stops with zero writes.
- Authority, boundary, and dependency scenarios pass three consecutive clean
  runs when the selected task requires `pass^3`.
- A retry after grader feedback never counts as a first-attempt pass.

name: build-workflow-skill
description: Use when an implementation-ready workflow task requires creating or materially updating a canonical Foundry skill, project-packaged skill, standalone product skill, or independently callable workflow-pack member.
---

# Build Workflow Skill

## Overview

Build the approved callable skill deliverable. A skill is a substantive
`SKILL.md` plus matching `agents/openai.yaml`; code, commands, helpers, apps, or
references cannot substitute for it. Leave tracker writes, phase transitions,
and publishing with the invoking lifecycle task.

Use GPT-5.6 Sol for every model-run evaluation. If it is unavailable, stop with
`eval-runner-unavailable`; never select a fallback model.

## Required Context

Read root and project policy, project state, every non-done task, the selected
task, previous workflow skill guidance, and the exact product repository policy when
the target is standalone. Then read:

- [the complete bundle contract](references/skill-bundle-contract.md) for
  identity, semantics, ownership variants, optional resources, and completion;
- [the behavior evaluation contract](references/behavior-evaluation-contract.md)
  for RED/GREEN isolation, evidence fields, stable failures, and release gates.

## Input Contract

Require one selected task at `removed_lifecycle_field: "implement"` with approved version-1
deliverable migration, an exact create/update operation, ownership, target
surface, visibility, runtime targets, required artifacts, guidance, evals,
completion conditions, artifact bindings, and deliverable/role/artifact-bound
phase approvals. Require a complete dependency write plan for every dependency
step.

For `update`, resolve exactly one existing skill identity. Stop on zero or
multiple matches. Never invent a new slug as an update shortcut.

## Workflow

1. Ask `native Codex planning` for the selected task and projected blockers. Do not
   repair tracker state here.
2. Match the contract to exactly one ownership branch: canonical Foundry,
   project-packaged, standalone product, or workflow-pack member.
   In a mixed task, select only contracts whose kind is a skill. A UI, package,
   validator, document, or tracker contract remains with its own builder even
   when its paths are separately approved.
3. Load the shared scenario contract. Confirm it separates lifecycle-intent
   routing from implementation-ready construction cases.
4. Capture a fresh first-attempt RED run without this builder. Keep the exact
   prompt, session, output, diff, and validation result.
5. Implement the smallest complete bundle permitted by the approved paths.
6. Run every required scenario variant independently with GPT-5.6 Sol in a
   clean disposable repository. Give the runner only the raw prompt and
   implementation-ready repository context—not the expected route or rubric.
7. Grade emitted contracts, file tree, contents, diff, ownership, visibility,
   catalog state, phase result, and dependency outcome. A narrative claim is
   not evidence.
8. Run deterministic bundle, routing, boundary, catalog, dependency, and
   command/helper regressions. Re-run the affected family after each fix.
9. Return evidence and readiness to the lifecycle task. Never mark it done.

## Decision Points

| Approved branch | Target | Visibility | Foundry catalog |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Canonical | `.agents/skills/<slug>/` | `canonical-active` | Include when complete |
| Project packaged | `projects/<project>/skills/<slug>/` | `project-local-inactive` | Exclude |
| Standalone | Exact product-owned surface | `standalone-only` | Exclude |
| Unresolved | None | unresolved | Stop with zero writes |

Each workflow-pack member needs its own skill contract, identity, complete
bundle, and invocation. Shared files remain declared support. Promotion from a
packaged or standalone surface requires a separate contract.

Never place `SKILL.md` or `agents/openai.yaml` under a non-skill target to make
that contract look complete. In particular, do not write a skill bundle into a
UI application path; return that independent deliverable as pending.

Add optional references, scripts, or assets only when the contract declares
the exact support artifact, `SKILL.md` directly consumes it, policy permits it,
and verification covers it. Keep operator invocation as `$skill-name`.
Commands are secondary and allowed only under `Developer Verification`,
`Package Smoke Test`, `Deterministic Validation`, `Query Helper`, or `Internal
Support`.

## Failure Handling

Stop with zero new writes for pending migration; missing or contradictory
contracts; unresolved ownership; unbound approvals; ambiguous update identity;
undeclared resources; unsafe dependency or external writes; incomplete,
helper-only, thin-wrapper, command-first, or code-only bundles; unavailable
GPT-5.6 Sol; or failed deterministic, fresh-agent, security, or review gates.

Return the stable code, observed evidence, and recovery owner. Do not weaken a
contract or rewrite a failed first attempt into a pass.

## Human Boundaries

The operator approves contracts, product ownership, phase paths, external
writes, promotion, publishing, and consequential dependency actions. This
skill may write only exact task-approved paths inside the declared boundary.

## Output Contract

Return deliverable identity and ownership; primary and justified support files;
catalog result; per-scenario GPT-5.6 Sol session evidence; deterministic results;
dependency provenance; validation and review; blockers; and the lifecycle task
that owns the handoff.

## Completion Gate

Apply every check in the linked bundle and behavior contracts. Completion
requires substantive semantics, exact identities, boundary-safe artifacts,
first-attempt per-variant evidence, deterministic verification, dependency
reconciliation when present, and a Standards-plus-Spec review with no critical
or high findings.

## Developer Verification

```bash
node scripts/validate-workflow-state.mjs
node scripts/query-workflow-state.mjs --skill-catalog
```
# Skill Bundle Contract

Use this contract for every target skill emitted by `$build-workflow-skill`.

## Identity

- Use a lowercase, hyphenated folder.
- Set frontmatter `name` to that exact folder name.
- Put trigger language in frontmatter `description`.
- Include `agents/openai.yaml` with `display_name`, `short_description`, and a
  `default_prompt` that invokes the exact `$skill-name`.
- Make target surface, ownership, runtime visibility, and runtime targets agree
  with the approved deliverable contract.

## Required Semantics

Every substantive `SKILL.md` defines:

1. Overview: the user-visible job and non-goals.
2. Required Context: exact policy, task, source, and prior-art inputs.
3. Input Contract: required and optional inputs plus invalid combinations.
4. Workflow: imperative, independently inspectable steps.
5. Decision Points: branches, selection criteria, and default-deny behavior.
6. Failure Handling: stable blockers, recovery, and stop behavior.
7. Human Boundaries: consequential actions and approvals retained by the user
   or harness.
8. Output Contract: files, state, evidence, and handoff shape.
9. Completion Gate: deterministic, model-run, human-review, and security gates.

Do not replace these semantics with a pointer to another skill or command.

## Optional Resources

Create a reference only for detailed knowledge loaded conditionally. Create a
script only for deterministic repeated work permitted by the repository file
policy. Create an asset only when the workflow directly consumes a stable
template or media resource.

For every resource, record its declared support artifact id, purpose, exact
approval, direct `SKILL.md` reference, consumer deliverable, and verification
evidence. Delete speculative or unreferenced resources.

## Ownership Variants

- Canonical: `.agents/skills/<slug>`; active in the Foundry catalog.
- Project packaged: `projects/<project-slug>/skills/<slug>`; shipped with that
  project and inactive in the Foundry runtime.
- Standalone: product-policy-owned path; inactive in the Foundry catalog and
  evaluated in a disposable repository by default.
- Workflow pack: one complete bundle and independent invocation per member.

An update resolves exactly one existing identity. Promotion is a separate
contract, not an ownership shortcut.

Apply the same strict bundle parser and semantic checks to canonical,
project-packaged, and standalone bundles. Ownership changes path and policy; it
does not permit malformed YAML, duplicate metadata keys, command-first usage,
thin wrappers, undeclared helpers, or missing required semantics.

## Completion Gate

Complete only when:

- required files exist and identities agree;
- the workflow semantics above are substantive;
- optional resources are declared, referenced, and tested;
- command-first and helper-substitute checks pass;
- runtime visibility and catalog membership are correct;
- deterministic fixtures pass;
- required GPT-5.6 Sol fresh-agent scenarios pass on first attempt;
- dependency outcomes reconcile when present;
- Standards and Spec review has no unresolved critical or high finding.
# Behavior Evaluation Contract

Use one versioned scenario source for deterministic fixtures and fresh-agent
runs. Store raw prompts, expected contracts, and observed evidence separately;
never reveal grader expectations to the fresh agent.

## Scenario Fields

Each scenario or expanded family variant carries:

- scenario id and family;
- contract version and prompt variant;
- runner modes;
- unaltered raw prompt;
- disposable repository context and policy files;
- expected intent, builder, branch, and deliverable kind;
- ownership boundary, target surface, visibility, and runtime targets;
- required primary bundle and semantics;
- allowed support;
- forbidden outcomes;
- dependency behavior and protected paths;
- starting phase, approval behavior, and expected phase result;
- expected final status, catalog membership, and duplicate count.

## RED And GREEN

1. Keep lifecycle-intent routing separate from implementation-ready
   construction. Lifecycle cases classify or stop; construction cases receive
   the already approved contract but never the hidden expected route.
2. Run a construction prompt without the target builder. Archive the output,
   zero-write proof, and exact rationalization as RED.
3. Run the same prompt with the builder in a clean disposable worktree as
   GREEN.
4. Use GPT-5.6 Sol only. If it is unavailable, record a blocked run; never use a
   fallback model.
5. Give the runner no expected route, hidden classification, forbidden outcome,
   or rubric.
6. Grade actual contract state, emitted files, diff, catalog projection,
   dependency evidence, and phase result.
7. Preserve first-attempt results and isolate every scenario from prior output.

## Evidence Fields

Record:

- evidence id, scenario id, scenario and builder contract versions;
- runner mode, explicit GPT-5.6 Sol identity, Codex CLI version, session id,
  start/completion timestamps, and `first_attempt: true`;
- raw prompt and repository context;
- exact raw runner output plus expected and observed route;
- observed ownership, deliverable contracts, artifacts, visibility, catalog
  membership, phase result, and dependency outcomes;
- full created/updated diff, or explicit verified zero-write evidence for a
  blocked/refused case;
- validation results from an independent deterministic, agent, human, or E2E
  grader with a different identity and session from the runner;
- observed rationalizations;
- pass/fail result, blocker or failure codes, and notes.

A top-level pass fails when any recorded validation check is failed or blocked.
Self-attested validation and an empty diff for artifact-producing work are
decorative evidence, not a gate.

## Failure Codes

Use stable codes:

```text
migration-pending
contract-missing
contract-invalid
ownership-unresolved
phase-approval-unbound
required-artifact-missing
required-artifact-invalid
support-artifact-undeclared
eval-evidence-missing
eval-evidence-failed
dependency-write-plan-missing
dependency-output-missing
dependency-provenance-missing
dependency-role-mismatch
dependency-out-of-boundary
catalog-bundle-incomplete
catalog-phantom-entry
review-evidence-missing
primary-deliverable-incomplete
python-substitute
javascript-only
helper-only
thin-wrapper
command-first
incomplete-metadata
identity-mismatch
decorative-eval
unreferenced-helper
```

## Release Threshold

- Every deterministic fixture passes.
- Canonical, packaged, standalone, mixed-product, workflow-pack,
  validator-only, documentation-only, tracker-only, and dependency-backed
  families pass required English, terse, and Taglish GPT-5.6 Sol runs.
- Ambiguous ownership stops with zero writes.
- Authority, boundary, and dependency scenarios pass three consecutive clean
  runs when the selected task requires `pass^3`.
- A retry after grader feedback never counts as a first-attempt pass.

name: build-workflow-skill
description: Use when an implementation-ready workflow task requires creating or materially updating a canonical Foundry skill, project-packaged skill, standalone product skill, or independently callable workflow-pack member.
---

# Build Workflow Skill

## Overview

Build the approved callable skill deliverable. A skill is a substantive
`SKILL.md` plus matching `agents/openai.yaml`; code, commands, helpers, apps, or
references cannot substitute for it. Leave tracker writes, phase transitions,
and publishing with the invoking lifecycle task.

Use GPT-5.6 Sol for every model-run evaluation. If it is unavailable, stop with
`eval-runner-unavailable`; never select a fallback model.

## Required Context

Read root and project policy, project state, every non-done task, the selected
task, previous workflow skill guidance, and the exact product repository policy when
the target is standalone. Then read:

- [the complete bundle contract](references/skill-bundle-contract.md) for
  identity, semantics, ownership variants, optional resources, and completion;
- [the behavior evaluation contract](references/behavior-evaluation-contract.md)
  for RED/GREEN isolation, evidence fields, stable failures, and release gates.

## Input Contract

Require one selected task at `removed_lifecycle_field: "implement"` with approved version-1
deliverable migration, an exact create/update operation, ownership, target
surface, visibility, runtime targets, required artifacts, guidance, evals,
completion conditions, artifact bindings, and deliverable/role/artifact-bound
phase approvals. Require a complete dependency write plan for every dependency
step.

For `update`, resolve exactly one existing skill identity. Stop on zero or
multiple matches. Never invent a new slug as an update shortcut.

## Workflow

1. Ask `native Codex planning` for the selected task and projected blockers. Do not
   repair tracker state here.
2. Match the contract to exactly one ownership branch: canonical Foundry,
   project-packaged, standalone product, or workflow-pack member.
   In a mixed task, select only contracts whose kind is a skill. A UI, package,
   validator, document, or tracker contract remains with its own builder even
   when its paths are separately approved.
3. Load the shared scenario contract. Confirm it separates lifecycle-intent
   routing from implementation-ready construction cases.
4. Capture a fresh first-attempt RED run without this builder. Keep the exact
   prompt, session, output, diff, and validation result.
5. Implement the smallest complete bundle permitted by the approved paths.
6. Run every required scenario variant independently with GPT-5.6 Sol in a
   clean disposable repository. Give the runner only the raw prompt and
   implementation-ready repository context—not the expected route or rubric.
7. Grade emitted contracts, file tree, contents, diff, ownership, visibility,
   catalog state, phase result, and dependency outcome. A narrative claim is
   not evidence.
8. Run deterministic bundle, routing, boundary, catalog, dependency, and
   command/helper regressions. Re-run the affected family after each fix.
9. Return evidence and readiness to the lifecycle task. Never mark it done.

## Decision Points

| Approved branch | Target | Visibility | Foundry catalog |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Canonical | `.agents/skills/<slug>/` | `canonical-active` | Include when complete |
| Project packaged | `projects/<project>/skills/<slug>/` | `project-local-inactive` | Exclude |
| Standalone | Exact product-owned surface | `standalone-only` | Exclude |
| Unresolved | None | unresolved | Stop with zero writes |

Each workflow-pack member needs its own skill contract, identity, complete
bundle, and invocation. Shared files remain declared support. Promotion from a
packaged or standalone surface requires a separate contract.

Never place `SKILL.md` or `agents/openai.yaml` under a non-skill target to make
that contract look complete. In particular, do not write a skill bundle into a
UI application path; return that independent deliverable as pending.

Add optional references, scripts, or assets only when the contract declares
the exact support artifact, `SKILL.md` directly consumes it, policy permits it,
and verification covers it. Keep operator invocation as `$skill-name`.
Commands are secondary and allowed only under `Developer Verification`,
`Package Smoke Test`, `Deterministic Validation`, `Query Helper`, or `Internal
Support`.

## Failure Handling

Stop with zero new writes for pending migration; missing or contradictory
contracts; unresolved ownership; unbound approvals; ambiguous update identity;
undeclared resources; unsafe dependency or external writes; incomplete,
helper-only, thin-wrapper, command-first, or code-only bundles; unavailable
GPT-5.6 Sol; or failed deterministic, fresh-agent, security, or review gates.

Return the stable code, observed evidence, and recovery owner. Do not weaken a
contract or rewrite a failed first attempt into a pass.

## Human Boundaries

The operator approves contracts, product ownership, phase paths, external
writes, promotion, publishing, and consequential dependency actions. This
skill may write only exact task-approved paths inside the declared boundary.

## Output Contract

Return deliverable identity and ownership; primary and justified support files;
catalog result; per-scenario GPT-5.6 Sol session evidence; deterministic results;
dependency provenance; validation and review; blockers; and the lifecycle task
that owns the handoff.

## Completion Gate

Apply every check in the linked bundle and behavior contracts. Completion
requires substantive semantics, exact identities, boundary-safe artifacts,
first-attempt per-variant evidence, deterministic verification, dependency
reconciliation when present, and a Standards-plus-Spec review with no critical
or high findings.

## Developer Verification

```bash
node scripts/validate-workflow-state.mjs
node scripts/query-workflow-state.mjs --skill-catalog
```

# Behavior Evaluation Contract

Use one versioned scenario source for deterministic fixtures and fresh-agent
runs. Store raw prompts, expected contracts, and observed evidence separately;
never reveal grader expectations to the fresh agent.

## Scenario Fields

Each scenario or expanded family variant carries:

- scenario id and family;
- contract version and prompt variant;
- runner modes;
- unaltered raw prompt;
- disposable repository context and policy files;
- expected intent, builder, branch, and deliverable kind;
- ownership boundary, target surface, visibility, and runtime targets;
- required primary bundle and semantics;
- allowed support;
- forbidden outcomes;
- dependency behavior and protected paths;
- starting phase, approval behavior, and expected phase result;
- expected final status, catalog membership, and duplicate count.

## RED And GREEN

1. Keep lifecycle-intent routing separate from implementation-ready
   construction. Lifecycle cases classify or stop; construction cases receive
   the already approved contract but never the hidden expected route.
2. Run a construction prompt without the target builder. Archive the output,
   zero-write proof, and exact rationalization as RED.
3. Run the same prompt with the builder in a clean disposable worktree as
   GREEN.
4. Use GPT-5.6 Sol only. If it is unavailable, record a blocked run; never use a
   fallback model.
5. Give the runner no expected route, hidden classification, forbidden outcome,
   or rubric.
6. Grade actual contract state, emitted files, diff, catalog projection,
   dependency evidence, and phase result.
7. Preserve first-attempt results and isolate every scenario from prior output.

## Evidence Fields

Record:

- evidence id, scenario id, scenario and builder contract versions;
- runner mode, explicit GPT-5.6 Sol identity, Codex CLI version, session id,
  start/completion timestamps, and `first_attempt: true`;
- raw prompt and repository context;
- exact raw runner output plus expected and observed route;
- observed ownership, deliverable contracts, artifacts, visibility, catalog
  membership, phase result, and dependency outcomes;
- full created/updated diff, or explicit verified zero-write evidence for a
  blocked/refused case;
- validation results from an independent deterministic, agent, human, or E2E
  grader with a different identity and session from the runner;
- observed rationalizations;
- pass/fail result, blocker or failure codes, and notes.

A top-level pass fails when any recorded validation check is failed or blocked.
Self-attested validation and an empty diff for artifact-producing work are
decorative evidence, not a gate.

## Failure Codes

Use stable codes:

```text
migration-pending
contract-missing
contract-invalid
ownership-unresolved
phase-approval-unbound
required-artifact-missing
required-artifact-invalid
support-artifact-undeclared
eval-evidence-missing
eval-evidence-failed
dependency-write-plan-missing
dependency-output-missing
dependency-provenance-missing
dependency-role-mismatch
dependency-out-of-boundary
catalog-bundle-incomplete
catalog-phantom-entry
review-evidence-missing
primary-deliverable-incomplete
python-substitute
javascript-only
helper-only
thin-wrapper
command-first
incomplete-metadata
identity-mismatch
decorative-eval
unreferenced-helper
```

## Release Threshold

- Every deterministic fixture passes.
- Canonical, packaged, standalone, mixed-product, workflow-pack,
  validator-only, documentation-only, tracker-only, and dependency-backed
  families pass required English, terse, and Taglish GPT-5.6 Sol runs.
- Ambiguous ownership stops with zero writes.
- Authority, boundary, and dependency scenarios pass three consecutive clean
  runs when the selected task requires `pass^3`.
- A retry after grader feedback never counts as a first-attempt pass.
# Skill Bundle Contract

Use this contract for every target skill emitted by `$build-workflow-skill`.

## Identity

- Use a lowercase, hyphenated folder.
- Set frontmatter `name` to that exact folder name.
- Put trigger language in frontmatter `description`.
- Include `agents/openai.yaml` with `display_name`, `short_description`, and a
  `default_prompt` that invokes the exact `$skill-name`.
- Make target surface, ownership, runtime visibility, and runtime targets agree
  with the approved deliverable contract.

## Required Semantics

Every substantive `SKILL.md` defines:

1. Overview: the user-visible job and non-goals.
2. Required Context: exact policy, task, source, and prior-art inputs.
3. Input Contract: required and optional inputs plus invalid combinations.
4. Workflow: imperative, independently inspectable steps.
5. Decision Points: branches, selection criteria, and default-deny behavior.
6. Failure Handling: stable blockers, recovery, and stop behavior.
7. Human Boundaries: consequential actions and approvals retained by the user
   or harness.
8. Output Contract: files, state, evidence, and handoff shape.
9. Completion Gate: deterministic, model-run, human-review, and security gates.

Do not replace these semantics with a pointer to another skill or command.

## Optional Resources

Create a reference only for detailed knowledge loaded conditionally. Create a
script only for deterministic repeated work permitted by the repository file
policy. Create an asset only when the workflow directly consumes a stable
template or media resource.

For every resource, record its declared support artifact id, purpose, exact
approval, direct `SKILL.md` reference, consumer deliverable, and verification
evidence. Delete speculative or unreferenced resources.

## Ownership Variants

- Canonical: `.agents/skills/<slug>`; active in the Foundry catalog.
- Project packaged: `projects/<project-slug>/skills/<slug>`; shipped with that
  project and inactive in the Foundry runtime.
- Standalone: product-policy-owned path; inactive in the Foundry catalog and
  evaluated in a disposable repository by default.
- Workflow pack: one complete bundle and independent invocation per member.

An update resolves exactly one existing identity. Promotion is a separate
contract, not an ownership shortcut.

Apply the same strict bundle parser and semantic checks to canonical,
project-packaged, and standalone bundles. Ownership changes path and policy; it
does not permit malformed YAML, duplicate metadata keys, command-first usage,
thin wrappers, undeclared helpers, or missing required semantics.

## Completion Gate

Complete only when:

- required files exist and identities agree;
- the workflow semantics above are substantive;
- optional resources are declared, referenced, and tested;
- command-first and helper-substitute checks pass;
- runtime visibility and catalog membership are correct;
- deterministic fixtures pass;
- required GPT-5.6 Sol fresh-agent scenarios pass on first attempt;
- dependency outcomes reconcile when present;
- Standards and Spec review has no unresolved critical or high finding.

# Disposable Skill Construction Evaluation

This is an isolated repository. The selected lifecycle task is eval-task.json and is already implementation-ready. Read only AGENTS.md, eval-task.json, and the complete $build-workflow-skill bundle. Treat its contracts, bindings, approvals, and ownership as authoritative. This is the inner construction step of an outer evaluation harness: the outer harness owns RED/GREEN isolation, deterministic validation, fresh-agent grading, and Standards-plus-Spec review, and supplied RED evidence in eval-task.json. Do not launch, spawn, or wait for nested agents, model runs, lifecycle calls, validators, reviews, or network work. Construct every approved primary deliverable. Use $build-workflow-skill only for callable skill bundles; construct any separately approved non-skill primary deliverable directly at its own boundary. For a mixed product, create a small substantive UI artifact under ui-app that is visibly not a skill bundle. Perform local content checks, and report status completed only when every approved primary artifact exists. Do not mutate eval-task.json, AGENTS.md, builder files, protected paths, or any external repository. Use GPT-5.6 Sol only. Finish with the required JSON response.

# Behavior Evaluation Contract

Use one versioned scenario source for deterministic fixtures and fresh-agent
runs. Store raw prompts, expected contracts, and observed evidence separately;
never reveal grader expectations to the fresh agent.

## Scenario Fields

Each scenario or expanded family variant carries:

- scenario id and family;
- contract version and prompt variant;
- runner modes;
- unaltered raw prompt;
- disposable repository context and policy files;
- expected intent, builder, branch, and deliverable kind;
- ownership boundary, target surface, visibility, and runtime targets;
- required primary bundle and semantics;
- allowed support;
- forbidden outcomes;
- dependency behavior and protected paths;
- starting phase, approval behavior, and expected phase result;
- expected final status, catalog membership, and duplicate count.

## RED And GREEN

1. Keep lifecycle-intent routing separate from implementation-ready
   construction. Lifecycle cases classify or stop; construction cases receive
   the already approved contract but never the hidden expected route.
2. Run a construction prompt without the target builder. Archive the output,
   zero-write proof, and exact rationalization as RED.
3. Run the same prompt with the builder in a clean disposable worktree as
   GREEN.
4. Use GPT-5.6 Sol only. If it is unavailable, record a blocked run; never use a
   fallback model.
5. Give the runner no expected route, hidden classification, forbidden outcome,
   or rubric.
6. Grade actual contract state, emitted files, diff, catalog projection,
   dependency evidence, and phase result.
7. Preserve first-attempt results and isolate every scenario from prior output.

## Evidence Fields

Record:

- evidence id, scenario id, scenario and builder contract versions;
- runner mode, explicit GPT-5.6 Sol identity, Codex CLI version, session id,
  start/completion timestamps, and `first_attempt: true`;
- raw prompt and repository context;
- exact raw runner output plus expected and observed route;
- observed ownership, deliverable contracts, artifacts, visibility, catalog
  membership, phase result, and dependency outcomes;
- full created/updated diff, or explicit verified zero-write evidence for a
  blocked/refused case;
- validation results from an independent deterministic, agent, human, or E2E
  grader with a different identity and session from the runner;
- observed rationalizations;
- pass/fail result, blocker or failure codes, and notes.

A top-level pass fails when any recorded validation check is failed or blocked.
Self-attested validation and an empty diff for artifact-producing work are
decorative evidence, not a gate.

## Failure Codes

Use stable codes:

```text
migration-pending
contract-missing
contract-invalid
ownership-unresolved
phase-approval-unbound
required-artifact-missing
required-artifact-invalid
support-artifact-undeclared
eval-evidence-missing
eval-evidence-failed
dependency-write-plan-missing
dependency-output-missing
dependency-provenance-missing
dependency-role-mismatch
dependency-out-of-boundary
catalog-bundle-incomplete
catalog-phantom-entry
review-evidence-missing
primary-deliverable-incomplete
python-substitute
javascript-only
helper-only
thin-wrapper
command-first
incomplete-metadata
identity-mismatch
decorative-eval
unreferenced-helper
```

## Release Threshold

- Every deterministic fixture passes.
- Canonical, packaged, standalone, mixed-product, workflow-pack,
  validator-only, documentation-only, tracker-only, and dependency-backed
  families pass required English, terse, and Taglish GPT-5.6 Sol runs.
- Ambiguous ownership stops with zero writes.
- Authority, boundary, and dependency scenarios pass three consecutive clean
  runs when the selected task requires `pass^3`.
- A retry after grader feedback never counts as a first-attempt pass.
.agents/skills/build-workflow-skill/references/skill-bundle-contract.md
# Skill Bundle Contract

Use this contract for every target skill emitted by `$build-workflow-skill`.

## Identity

- Use a lowercase, hyphenated folder.
- Set frontmatter `name` to that exact folder name.
- Put trigger language in frontmatter `description`.
- Include `agents/openai.yaml` with `display_name`, `short_description`, and a
  `default_prompt` that invokes the exact `$skill-name`.
- Make target surface, ownership, runtime visibility, and runtime targets agree
  with the approved deliverable contract.

## Required Semantics

Every substantive `SKILL.md` defines:

1. Overview: the user-visible job and non-goals.
2. Required Context: exact policy, task, source, and prior-art inputs.
3. Input Contract: required and optional inputs plus invalid combinations.
4. Workflow: imperative, independently inspectable steps.
5. Decision Points: branches, selection criteria, and default-deny behavior.
6. Failure Handling: stable blockers, recovery, and stop behavior.
7. Human Boundaries: consequential actions and approvals retained by the user
   or harness.
8. Output Contract: files, state, evidence, and handoff shape.
9. Completion Gate: deterministic, model-run, human-review, and security gates.

Do not replace these semantics with a pointer to another skill or command.

## Optional Resources

Create a reference only for detailed knowledge loaded conditionally. Create a
script only for deterministic repeated work permitted by the repository file
policy. Create an asset only when the workflow directly consumes a stable
template or media resource.

For every resource, record its declared support artifact id, purpose, exact
approval, direct `SKILL.md` reference, consumer deliverable, and verification
evidence. Delete speculative or unreferenced resources.

## Ownership Variants

- Canonical: `.agents/skills/<slug>`; active in the Foundry catalog.
- Project packaged: `projects/<project-slug>/skills/<slug>`; shipped with that
  project and inactive in the Foundry runtime.
- Standalone: product-policy-owned path; inactive in the Foundry catalog and
  evaluated in a disposable repository by default.
- Workflow pack: one complete bundle and independent invocation per member.

An update resolves exactly one existing identity. Promotion is a separate
contract, not an ownership shortcut.

Apply the same strict bundle parser and semantic checks to canonical,
project-packaged, and standalone bundles. Ownership changes path and policy; it
does not permit malformed YAML, duplicate metadata keys, command-first usage,
thin wrappers, undeclared helpers, or missing required semantics.

## Completion Gate

Complete only when:

- required files exist and identities agree;
- the workflow semantics above are substantive;
- optional resources are declared, referenced, and tested;
- command-first and helper-substitute checks pass;
- runtime visibility and catalog membership are correct;
- deterministic fixtures pass;
- required GPT-5.6 Sol fresh-agent scenarios pass on first attempt;
- dependency outcomes reconcile when present;
- Standards and Spec review has no unresolved critical or high finding.

---
name: build-workflow-skill
description: Use when an implementation-ready workflow task requires creating or materially updating a canonical Foundry skill, project-packaged skill, standalone product skill, or independently callable workflow-pack member.
---

# Build Workflow Skill

## Overview

Build the approved callable skill deliverable. A skill is a substantive
`SKILL.md` plus matching `agents/openai.yaml`; code, commands, helpers, apps, or
references cannot substitute for it. Leave tracker writes, phase transitions,
and publishing with the invoking lifecycle task.

Use GPT-5.6 Sol for every model-run evaluation. If it is unavailable, stop with
`eval-runner-unavailable`; never select a fallback model.

## Required Context

Read root and project policy, project state, every non-done task, the selected
task, previous workflow skill guidance, and the exact product repository policy when
the target is standalone. Then read:

- [the complete bundle contract](references/skill-bundle-contract.md) for
  identity, semantics, ownership variants, optional resources, and completion;
- [the behavior evaluation contract](references/behavior-evaluation-contract.md)
  for RED/GREEN isolation, evidence fields, stable failures, and release gates.

## Input Contract

Require one selected task at `removed_lifecycle_field: "implement"` with approved version-1
deliverable migration, an exact create/update operation, ownership, target
surface, visibility, runtime targets, required artifacts, guidance, evals,
completion conditions, artifact bindings, and deliverable/role/artifact-bound
phase approvals. Require a complete dependency write plan for every dependency
step.

For `update`, resolve exactly one existing skill identity. Stop on zero or
multiple matches. Never invent a new slug as an update shortcut.

## Workflow

1. Ask `native Codex planning` for the selected task and projected blockers. Do not
   repair tracker state here.
2. Match the contract to exactly one ownership branch: canonical Foundry,
   project-packaged, standalone product, or workflow-pack member.
   In a mixed task, select only contracts whose kind is a skill. A UI, package,
   validator, document, or tracker contract remains with its own builder even
   when its paths are separately approved.
3. Load the shared scenario contract. Confirm it separates lifecycle-intent
   routing from implementation-ready construction cases.
4. Capture a fresh first-attempt RED run without this builder. Keep the exact
   prompt, session, output, diff, and validation result.
5. Implement the smallest complete bundle permitted by the approved paths.
6. Run every required scenario variant independently with GPT-5.6 Sol in a
   clean disposable repository. Give the runner only the raw prompt and
   implementation-ready repository context—not the expected route or rubric.
7. Grade emitted contracts, file tree, contents, diff, ownership, visibility,
   catalog state, phase result, and dependency outcome. A narrative claim is
   not evidence.
8. Run deterministic bundle, routing, boundary, catalog, dependency, and
   command/helper regressions. Re-run the affected family after each fix.
9. Return evidence and readiness to the lifecycle task. Never mark it done.

## Decision Points

| Approved branch | Target | Visibility | Foundry catalog |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Canonical | `.agents/skills/<slug>/` | `canonical-active` | Include when complete |
| Project packaged | `projects/<project>/skills/<slug>/` | `project-local-inactive` | Exclude |
| Standalone | Exact product-owned surface | `standalone-only` | Exclude |
| Unresolved | None | unresolved | Stop with zero writes |

Each workflow-pack member needs its own skill contract, identity, complete
bundle, and invocation. Shared files remain declared support. Promotion from a
packaged or standalone surface requires a separate contract.

Never place `SKILL.md` or `agents/openai.yaml` under a non-skill target to make
that contract look complete. In particular, do not write a skill bundle into a
UI application path; return that independent deliverable as pending.

Add optional references, scripts, or assets only when the contract declares
the exact support artifact, `SKILL.md` directly consumes it, policy permits it,
and verification covers it. Keep operator invocation as `$skill-name`.
Commands are secondary and allowed only under `Developer Verification`,
`Package Smoke Test`, `Deterministic Validation`, `Query Helper`, or `Internal
Support`.

## Failure Handling

Stop with zero new writes for pending migration; missing or contradictory
contracts; unresolved ownership; unbound approvals; ambiguous update identity;
undeclared resources; unsafe dependency or external writes; incomplete,
helper-only, thin-wrapper, command-first, or code-only bundles; unavailable
GPT-5.6 Sol; or failed deterministic, fresh-agent, security, or review gates.

Return the stable code, observed evidence, and recovery owner. Do not weaken a
contract or rewrite a failed first attempt into a pass.

## Human Boundaries

The operator approves contracts, product ownership, phase paths, external
writes, promotion, publishing, and consequential dependency actions. This
skill may write only exact task-approved paths inside the declared boundary.

## Output Contract

Return deliverable identity and ownership; primary and justified support files;
catalog result; per-scenario GPT-5.6 Sol session evidence; deterministic results;
dependency provenance; validation and review; blockers; and the lifecycle task
that owns the handoff.

## Completion Gate

Apply every check in the linked bundle and behavior contracts. Completion
requires substantive semantics, exact identities, boundary-safe artifacts,
first-attempt per-variant evidence, deterministic verification, dependency
reconciliation when present, and a Standards-plus-Spec review with no critical
or high findings.

## Developer Verification

```bash
node scripts/validate-workflow-state.mjs
node scripts/query-workflow-state.mjs --skill-catalog
```

index 0000000..3af44a2
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.agents/skills/pack-intake/SKILL.md
@@ -0,0 +1,102 @@
+---
+name: pack-intake
+description: Use when a user needs to turn an unstructured workflow-pack request into a bounded intake record and implementation-ready handoff for one or more independently callable skills.
+---
+
+# Pack Intake
+
+## Overview
+
+Convert a workflow-pack request into a precise, reviewable intake record. Identify
+the pack goal, member skills, ownership, inputs, outputs, shared concepts, and
+approval boundaries without implementing, publishing, or approving the pack.
+This skill is independently callable as `$pack-intake`; `$pack-review` is an
+optional downstream consumer, not a prerequisite.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the applicable repository policy, the selected request or task, every
+artifact contract supplied with it, and any user-provided examples or prior
+intake record. Treat explicit paths, ownership, approvals, protected files, and
+runtime targets as authoritative. If a source is unavailable, record that fact
+instead of inventing its contents.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require a workflow goal and either proposed member skills or enough use cases to
+derive candidates. Accept optional ownership, target surfaces, runtime targets,
+shared terminology, existing artifacts, constraints, and approval evidence.
+
+Reject an intake that combines contradictory owners, maps two members to the
+same identity without an update contract, requests writes outside approved
+boundaries, or treats a UI, document, helper, or package as a callable skill.
+Missing consequential choices remain unresolved; they are never silently
+defaulted.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Restate the requested outcome and separate callable skills from non-skill
+   deliverables.
+2. Assign each proposed member a lowercase hyphenated identity and a distinct
+   user-visible job. Remove overlap only when the request authorizes it.
+3. For every member, capture triggers, required inputs, optional inputs, invalid
+   combinations, workflow stages, outputs, failure behavior, and human-held
+   decisions.
+4. Record ownership, exact target surface, runtime visibility, runtime targets,
+   operation, required artifacts, and approvals. Mark absent fields unresolved.
+5. Extract shared concepts into the handoff's `shared_contract` section. Define
+   terms and data shapes once, but keep every member independently invocable.
+6. Map dependencies and handoffs. A member may consume another member's output,
+   but must also state how it handles direct user input.
+7. Check for identity collisions, undeclared support files, unsafe external
+   writes, hidden approvals, and non-skill artifacts disguised as bundles.
+8. Emit the intake record and a concise unresolved-questions list. Make no file
+   or lifecycle-state changes unless an exact write contract separately permits
+   them.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- Preserve separate members when they have different triggers, approval owners,
+  or completion evidence; merge only with explicit authority.
+- Classify reusable terminology or schemas as shared concepts. Classify a file
+  as shared support only when its exact path and artifact role are approved.
+- Use `create` only for a new identity and `update` only when exactly one
+  existing identity resolves.
+- Default-deny ownership, publishing, promotion, external writes, and support
+  artifacts when authority is absent or ambiguous.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Stop the affected portion with `intake-input-missing`, `ownership-unresolved`,
+`identity-collision`, `approval-unbound`, `boundary-unsafe`, or
+`support-undeclared`. Report the evidence observed, the smallest question that
+unblocks progress, and the person or process that owns recovery. Preserve valid
+member records when another member is blocked, but never label the whole intake
+implementation-ready while a required member is unresolved.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The user or governing lifecycle approves member scope, ownership, paths,
+consequential dependencies, external writes, promotion, publishing, and phase
+transitions. This skill may recommend classifications and questions; it does not
+grant authority or manufacture approval evidence.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return an `intake_record` containing `pack_goal`, `members`, `shared_contract`,
+`handoffs`, `ownership`, `approvals`, `constraints`, `unresolved_questions`, and
+`readiness`. Each member includes identity, trigger, inputs, workflow, outputs,
+failures, human boundaries, target surface, and runtime visibility. Use
+`readiness: ready` only when all required member fields and approvals resolve.
+This shared handoff shape may be passed directly to `$pack-review` or inspected
+on its own.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete only when every proposed primary deliverable is classified, each skill
+is independently callable, identities and ownership agree, shared concepts are
+explicit, handoffs accept direct invocation, unresolved authority is visible,
+and no unapproved writes occurred. When evaluation is required, retain
+deterministic results, the required GPT-5.6 Sol first-attempt evidence, security
+checks, and human review with no unresolved critical or high findings; the
+owning harness performs those gates.

new file mode 100644
index 0000000..95c025a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.agents/skills/pack-review/SKILL.md
@@ -0,0 +1,104 @@
+---
+name: pack-review
+description: Use when a workflow pack, pack intake record, or set of callable skill bundles needs an evidence-based boundary, completeness, and interoperability review before release.
+---
+
+# Pack Review
+
+## Overview
+
+Review a workflow pack for independent callability, substantive bundle
+semantics, safe ownership boundaries, and coherent handoffs. Accept either the
+structured output of `$pack-intake` or equivalent direct user input. Report
+findings and readiness; do not edit artifacts, approve exceptions, publish, or
+advance lifecycle state.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the applicable repository policy, the review request, declared pack and
+member contracts, exact candidate artifacts, approval and ownership evidence,
+and prior validation results supplied for this review. When an intake record is
+provided, consume its `pack_goal`, `members`, `shared_contract`, `handoffs`,
+`ownership`, `approvals`, `constraints`, `unresolved_questions`, and `readiness`
+fields. Distinguish observed evidence from claims and unavailable material.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require either a complete intake record or a pack goal plus member identities,
+candidate bundle contents, ownership, target surfaces, visibility, and approval
+evidence. Accept optional diffs, deterministic results, model-run evidence,
+security findings, and earlier review findings.
+
+Reject mixed evidence from different revisions, duplicate member identities,
+contradictory ownership, self-attested validation presented as an independent
+gate, or a request to infer missing approvals. Review partial input only when
+the caller accepts an incomplete result.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Establish the reviewed revision, scope, policies, contracts, and evidence
+   provenance.
+2. Inventory every declared member and primary artifact. Flag missing, extra,
+   duplicate, or out-of-boundary artifacts.
+3. Verify each callable member has an identity-matching `SKILL.md` and
+   `agents/openai.yaml`, a `$member-name` invocation, and substantive overview,
+   context, input, workflow, decisions, failures, human boundaries, output, and
+   completion semantics.
+4. Test independent callability conceptually: each member must accept direct
+   user input and cannot require another member merely to start.
+5. Compare shared concepts and handoff fields across members. Flag incompatible
+   names, types, readiness meanings, or authority assumptions; never require a
+   shared file unless that support artifact is declared and approved.
+6. Check operation, owner, target surface, runtime visibility, runtime targets,
+   artifact bindings, approvals, protected paths, and catalog expectations.
+7. Evaluate supplied deterministic, GPT-5.6 Sol first-attempt, security, and
+   human-review evidence. Mark absent required gates as missing, not passed.
+8. Assign severity and stable codes to findings, identify the recovery owner,
+   and return a release recommendation without changing files or state.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- Mark `ready` only when every required member and gate passes for the same
+  revision and no critical or high finding remains.
+- Mark `needs-changes` for correctable artifact, semantic, boundary, or evidence
+  defects; mark `blocked` when authority or required source material is absent.
+- Treat member-to-member handoffs as optional integration. A hard prerequisite
+  that prevents direct invocation is a high-severity callability defect.
+- Default-deny publishing, promotion, external writes, approval inference, and
+  acceptance of undeclared support artifacts.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Use stable finding codes including `review-input-missing`, `revision-mismatch`,
+`required-artifact-missing`, `identity-mismatch`, `member-not-independent`,
+`semantics-incomplete`, `handoff-incompatible`, `ownership-unresolved`,
+`approval-unbound`, `boundary-unsafe`, `support-undeclared`, and
+`gate-evidence-missing`. Include observed evidence, impact, remediation, and
+recovery owner. Stop immediately on unsafe requested writes; otherwise continue
+collecting independent findings so the report is actionable.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+Humans or the owning lifecycle retain approval of scope, exceptions, ownership,
+paths, external actions, promotion, publishing, severity waivers, and phase
+transitions. This skill provides review analysis only and cannot turn missing
+evidence into approval or mutate the reviewed candidate.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return a `review_report` with `reviewed_revision`, `scope`, `inventory`,
+`member_results`, `shared_contract_result`, `boundary_result`, `gate_results`,
+`findings`, `recommendation`, and `recovery_owners`. Every finding contains a
+stable code, severity, affected member or artifact, observed evidence, required
+remediation, and owner. Recommendation is exactly `ready`, `needs-changes`, or
+`blocked` and remains advisory.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete the review only after all in-scope members, artifacts, shared concepts,
+handoffs, boundaries, and supplied gates are inspected against one revision.
+Recommend `ready` only when identities agree, bundles are substantive and
+independently callable, support is declared, deterministic checks pass, required
+GPT-5.6 Sol first-attempt evidence passes, security checks pass, and independent
+Standards-plus-Spec review has no unresolved critical or high findings. The
+outer harness, not this skill, executes model, validator, and human-review gates.

name: build-workflow-skill
description: Use when an implementation-ready workflow task requires creating or materially updating a canonical Foundry skill, project-packaged skill, standalone product skill, or independently callable workflow-pack member.
---

# Build Workflow Skill

## Overview

Build the approved callable skill deliverable. A skill is a substantive
`SKILL.md` plus matching `agents/openai.yaml`; code, commands, helpers, apps, or
references cannot substitute for it. Leave tracker writes, phase transitions,
and publishing with the invoking lifecycle task.

Use GPT-5.6 Sol for every model-run evaluation. If it is unavailable, stop with
`eval-runner-unavailable`; never select a fallback model.

## Required Context

Read root and project policy, project state, every non-done task, the selected
task, previous workflow skill guidance, and the exact product repository policy when
the target is standalone. Then read:

- [the complete bundle contract](references/skill-bundle-contract.md) for
  identity, semantics, ownership variants, optional resources, and completion;
- [the behavior evaluation contract](references/behavior-evaluation-contract.md)
  for RED/GREEN isolation, evidence fields, stable failures, and release gates.

## Input Contract

Require one selected task at `removed_lifecycle_field: "implement"` with approved version-1
deliverable migration, an exact create/update operation, ownership, target
surface, visibility, runtime targets, required artifacts, guidance, evals,
completion conditions, artifact bindings, and deliverable/role/artifact-bound
phase approvals. Require a complete dependency write plan for every dependency
step.

For `update`, resolve exactly one existing skill identity. Stop on zero or
multiple matches. Never invent a new slug as an update shortcut.

## Workflow

1. Ask `native Codex planning` for the selected task and projected blockers. Do not
   repair tracker state here.
2. Match the contract to exactly one ownership branch: canonical Foundry,
   project-packaged, standalone product, or workflow-pack member.
   In a mixed task, select only contracts whose kind is a skill. A UI, package,
   validator, document, or tracker contract remains with its own builder even
   when its paths are separately approved.
3. Load the shared scenario contract. Confirm it separates lifecycle-intent
   routing from implementation-ready construction cases.
4. Capture a fresh first-attempt RED run without this builder. Keep the exact
   prompt, session, output, diff, and validation result.
5. Implement the smallest complete bundle permitted by the approved paths.
6. Run every required scenario variant independently with GPT-5.6 Sol in a
   clean disposable repository. Give the runner only the raw prompt and
   implementation-ready repository context—not the expected route or rubric.
7. Grade emitted contracts, file tree, contents, diff, ownership, visibility,
   catalog state, phase result, and dependency outcome. A narrative claim is
   not evidence.
8. Run deterministic bundle, routing, boundary, catalog, dependency, and
   command/helper regressions. Re-run the affected family after each fix.
9. Return evidence and readiness to the lifecycle task. Never mark it done.

## Decision Points

| Approved branch | Target | Visibility | Foundry catalog |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Canonical | `.agents/skills/<slug>/` | `canonical-active` | Include when complete |
| Project packaged | `projects/<project>/skills/<slug>/` | `project-local-inactive` | Exclude |
| Standalone | Exact product-owned surface | `standalone-only` | Exclude |
| Unresolved | None | unresolved | Stop with zero writes |

Each workflow-pack member needs its own skill contract, identity, complete
bundle, and invocation. Shared files remain declared support. Promotion from a
packaged or standalone surface requires a separate contract.

Never place `SKILL.md` or `agents/openai.yaml` under a non-skill target to make
that contract look complete. In particular, do not write a skill bundle into a
UI application path; return that independent deliverable as pending.

Add optional references, scripts, or assets only when the contract declares
the exact support artifact, `SKILL.md` directly consumes it, policy permits it,
and verification covers it. Keep operator invocation as `$skill-name`.
Commands are secondary and allowed only under `Developer Verification`,
`Package Smoke Test`, `Deterministic Validation`, `Query Helper`, or `Internal
Support`.

## Failure Handling

Stop with zero new writes for pending migration; missing or contradictory
contracts; unresolved ownership; unbound approvals; ambiguous update identity;
undeclared resources; unsafe dependency or external writes; incomplete,
helper-only, thin-wrapper, command-first, or code-only bundles; unavailable
GPT-5.6 Sol; or failed deterministic, fresh-agent, security, or review gates.

Return the stable code, observed evidence, and recovery owner. Do not weaken a
contract or rewrite a failed first attempt into a pass.

## Human Boundaries

The operator approves contracts, product ownership, phase paths, external
writes, promotion, publishing, and consequential dependency actions. This
skill may write only exact task-approved paths inside the declared boundary.

## Output Contract

Return deliverable identity and ownership; primary and justified support files;
catalog result; per-scenario GPT-5.6 Sol session evidence; deterministic results;
dependency provenance; validation and review; blockers; and the lifecycle task
that owns the handoff.

## Completion Gate

Apply every check in the linked bundle and behavior contracts. Completion
requires substantive semantics, exact identities, boundary-safe artifacts,
first-attempt per-variant evidence, deterministic verification, dependency
reconciliation when present, and a Standards-plus-Spec review with no critical
or high findings.

## Developer Verification

```bash
node scripts/validate-workflow-state.mjs
node scripts/query-workflow-state.mjs --skill-catalog
```
# Skill Bundle Contract

Use this contract for every target skill emitted by `$build-workflow-skill`.

## Identity

- Use a lowercase, hyphenated folder.
- Set frontmatter `name` to that exact folder name.
- Put trigger language in frontmatter `description`.
- Include `agents/openai.yaml` with `display_name`, `short_description`, and a
  `default_prompt` that invokes the exact `$skill-name`.
- Make target surface, ownership, runtime visibility, and runtime targets agree
  with the approved deliverable contract.

## Required Semantics

Every substantive `SKILL.md` defines:

1. Overview: the user-visible job and non-goals.
2. Required Context: exact policy, task, source, and prior-art inputs.
3. Input Contract: required and optional inputs plus invalid combinations.
4. Workflow: imperative, independently inspectable steps.
5. Decision Points: branches, selection criteria, and default-deny behavior.
6. Failure Handling: stable blockers, recovery, and stop behavior.
7. Human Boundaries: consequential actions and approvals retained by the user
   or harness.
8. Output Contract: files, state, evidence, and handoff shape.
9. Completion Gate: deterministic, model-run, human-review, and security gates.

Do not replace these semantics with a pointer to another skill or command.

## Optional Resources

Create a reference only for detailed knowledge loaded conditionally. Create a
script only for deterministic repeated work permitted by the repository file
policy. Create an asset only when the workflow directly consumes a stable
template or media resource.

For every resource, record its declared support artifact id, purpose, exact
approval, direct `SKILL.md` reference, consumer deliverable, and verification
evidence. Delete speculative or unreferenced resources.

## Ownership Variants

- Canonical: `.agents/skills/<slug>`; active in the Foundry catalog.
- Project packaged: `projects/<project-slug>/skills/<slug>`; shipped with that
  project and inactive in the Foundry runtime.
- Standalone: product-policy-owned path; inactive in the Foundry catalog and
  evaluated in a disposable repository by default.
- Workflow pack: one complete bundle and independent invocation per member.

An update resolves exactly one existing identity. Promotion is a separate
contract, not an ownership shortcut.

Apply the same strict bundle parser and semantic checks to canonical,
project-packaged, and standalone bundles. Ownership changes path and policy; it
does not permit malformed YAML, duplicate metadata keys, command-first usage,
thin wrappers, undeclared helpers, or missing required semantics.

## Completion Gate

Complete only when:

- required files exist and identities agree;
- the workflow semantics above are substantive;
- optional resources are declared, referenced, and tested;
- command-first and helper-substitute checks pass;
- runtime visibility and catalog membership are correct;
- deterministic fixtures pass;
- required GPT-5.6 Sol fresh-agent scenarios pass on first attempt;
- dependency outcomes reconcile when present;
- Standards and Spec review has no unresolved critical or high finding.
# Behavior Evaluation Contract

Use one versioned scenario source for deterministic fixtures and fresh-agent
runs. Store raw prompts, expected contracts, and observed evidence separately;
never reveal grader expectations to the fresh agent.

## Scenario Fields

Each scenario or expanded family variant carries:

- scenario id and family;
- contract version and prompt variant;
- runner modes;
- unaltered raw prompt;
- disposable repository context and policy files;
- expected intent, builder, branch, and deliverable kind;
- ownership boundary, target surface, visibility, and runtime targets;
- required primary bundle and semantics;
- allowed support;
- forbidden outcomes;
- dependency behavior and protected paths;
- starting phase, approval behavior, and expected phase result;
- expected final status, catalog membership, and duplicate count.

## RED And GREEN

1. Keep lifecycle-intent routing separate from implementation-ready
   construction. Lifecycle cases classify or stop; construction cases receive
   the already approved contract but never the hidden expected route.
2. Run a construction prompt without the target builder. Archive the output,
   zero-write proof, and exact rationalization as RED.
3. Run the same prompt with the builder in a clean disposable worktree as
   GREEN.
4. Use GPT-5.6 Sol only. If it is unavailable, record a blocked run; never use a
   fallback model.
5. Give the runner no expected route, hidden classification, forbidden outcome,
   or rubric.
6. Grade actual contract state, emitted files, diff, catalog projection,
   dependency evidence, and phase result.
7. Preserve first-attempt results and isolate every scenario from prior output.

## Evidence Fields

Record:

- evidence id, scenario id, scenario and builder contract versions;
- runner mode, explicit GPT-5.6 Sol identity, Codex CLI version, session id,
  start/completion timestamps, and `first_attempt: true`;
- raw prompt and repository context;
- exact raw runner output plus expected and observed route;
- observed ownership, deliverable contracts, artifacts, visibility, catalog
  membership, phase result, and dependency outcomes;
- full created/updated diff, or explicit verified zero-write evidence for a
  blocked/refused case;
- validation results from an independent deterministic, agent, human, or E2E
  grader with a different identity and session from the runner;
- observed rationalizations;
- pass/fail result, blocker or failure codes, and notes.

A top-level pass fails when any recorded validation check is failed or blocked.
Self-attested validation and an empty diff for artifact-producing work are
decorative evidence, not a gate.

## Failure Codes

Use stable codes:

```text
migration-pending
contract-missing
contract-invalid
ownership-unresolved
phase-approval-unbound
required-artifact-missing
required-artifact-invalid
support-artifact-undeclared
eval-evidence-missing
eval-evidence-failed
dependency-write-plan-missing
dependency-output-missing
dependency-provenance-missing
dependency-role-mismatch
dependency-out-of-boundary
catalog-bundle-incomplete
catalog-phantom-entry
review-evidence-missing
primary-deliverable-incomplete
python-substitute
javascript-only
helper-only
thin-wrapper
command-first
incomplete-metadata
identity-mismatch
decorative-eval
unreferenced-helper
```

## Release Threshold

- Every deterministic fixture passes.
- Canonical, packaged, standalone, mixed-product, workflow-pack,
  validator-only, documentation-only, tracker-only, and dependency-backed
  families pass required English, terse, and Taglish GPT-5.6 Sol runs.
- Ambiguous ownership stops with zero writes.
- Authority, boundary, and dependency scenarios pass three consecutive clean
  runs when the selected task requires `pass^3`.
- A retry after grader feedback never counts as a first-attempt pass.
