@@ -0,0 +1,146 @@
+---
+name: coverage-workflow
+description: Use when a coverage-product repository task requires inspecting test coverage, explaining uncovered behavior, or making a scoped coverage improvement with repository-native tests.
+---
+
+# Coverage Workflow
+
+## Overview
+
+Turn a coverage request in the coverage-product repository into an evidence-backed,
+reviewable result. Inspect the repository's own test and coverage conventions,
+identify behavior that lacks meaningful verification, and, when the request
+authorizes changes, add the smallest useful tests at the product-owned surface.
+
+Coverage percentages are diagnostic signals, not the goal. Do not add assertions
+that merely execute lines, change production behavior solely to inflate a metric,
+or publish reports, commits, pull requests, or external messages.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Before acting, read the repository policy files that govern the target, the selected
+task or user request, test-runner configuration, coverage configuration, package
+scripts, and the source and tests in scope. Inspect existing fixtures and nearby
+tests as prior art. When a baseline report is supplied, preserve its command,
+revision, thresholds, and environment so comparisons remain meaningful.
+
+Do not search unrelated repositories or load external coverage data unless the user
+explicitly places it in scope.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require:
+
+- a coverage-product repository worktree;
+- a requested mode: report, diagnose, or improve, inferred from explicit user
+  language when unambiguous;
+- an identifiable target such as a package, module, feature, changed-file set, or
+  repository-wide baseline; and
+- repository-supported commands or configuration sufficient to collect evidence.
+
+Optional inputs are a baseline artifact, target threshold, test filter, changed-file
+range, known flaky-test list, and explicit production-code authorization.
+
+Reject conflicting scopes, a threshold without a measurable target, reports from a
+different revision presented as comparable, or an improve request that would require
+unapproved production changes. A bare request defaults to diagnosis of the smallest
+repository-wide coverage command; it does not authorize edits.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Classify the request as report, diagnose, or improve and state the exact target.
+2. Resolve applicable repository policy and discover the native test and coverage
+   commands from checked-in configuration; do not invent a parallel harness.
+3. Record the worktree revision or equivalent state, relevant configuration,
+   command, filters, and runtime prerequisites.
+4. Run the narrowest representative baseline permitted by the request. Preserve the
+   exit status, summary metrics, threshold failures, skipped tests, and missing-data
+   warnings.
+5. Map uncovered statements, branches, functions, or lines back to user-visible
+   behaviors and failure paths. Distinguish genuinely missing tests from generated,
+   unreachable, platform-specific, or intentionally excluded code.
+6. In report mode, stop after presenting reproducible evidence. In diagnose mode,
+   rank gaps by behavioral risk, change frequency, and boundary importance, then
+   recommend concrete test cases.
+7. In improve mode, add or adjust repository-native tests for the highest-value
+   in-scope behavior. Prefer public interfaces and observable outcomes; cover error
+   handling and boundary branches when relevant. Modify production code only when
+   separately authorized and necessary for correctness, not test convenience.
+8. Run the focused tests first, then the applicable coverage command. Compare only
+   like-for-like results and inspect the diff for brittle fixtures, timing reliance,
+   excessive mocking, snapshots without intent, and unrelated changes.
+9. Hand off the result with commands, before/after evidence when available,
+   remaining gaps, limitations, and any decision still owned by the user.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+| Condition | Action |
+| --- | --- |
+| Report requested | Measure and explain; make no source or test edits. |
+| Diagnosis requested | Trace gaps to behaviors and propose prioritized cases; make no edits. |
+| Improvement explicitly requested | Edit only in-scope repository-native tests and approved production files. |
+| Coverage tool or configuration is absent | Stop and request the repository owner's choice of tooling. |
+| Baseline and candidate are not comparable | Report both separately; do not claim a delta. |
+| Threshold passes but critical behavior is untested | Report the risk; do not treat the percentage as completion. |
+| Required command is destructive, secret-dependent, or external | Stop before running it and request authorization or a safe fixture. |
+
+Default-deny edits, dependency installation, configuration changes, snapshot
+regeneration, network calls, and expansion beyond the selected target.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Use stable blockers in the handoff:
+
+- `coverage-scope-unresolved` when no single target can be identified;
+- `coverage-command-missing` when the repository has no supported measurement path;
+- `coverage-baseline-invalid` when evidence is incomplete or not attributable to
+  the current repository state;
+- `coverage-run-failed` when tests or instrumentation fail before usable results;
+- `coverage-threshold-failed` when a configured gate is not met;
+- `coverage-comparison-invalid` when before and after runs differ materially;
+- `coverage-change-unauthorized` when completion needs edits outside authorization;
+- `coverage-environment-blocked` when required services, secrets, or runtimes are
+  unavailable.
+
+Stop at the failing boundary, retain raw command and exit evidence, avoid presenting
+partial metrics as complete, and name the repository owner or user action that can
+recover the workflow. Never lower thresholds, broaden exclusions, delete tests, or
+silence failures to manufacture a pass.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The user retains approval for dependency changes, production-code changes not
+already requested, coverage threshold or exclusion changes, expensive or external
+test environments, generated artifact updates, publishing, commits, and pull
+requests. Repository owners decide whether risk warrants a test even when aggregate
+coverage already passes.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return:
+
+- mode, target, repository state, and configuration used;
+- exact test and coverage commands with exit results;
+- summary metrics and threshold status, without hiding skipped or missing data;
+- behavior-level findings with source and test locations;
+- for improve mode, the exact files changed and focused/full verification results;
+- comparable before/after deltas only when both runs share configuration and scope;
+- remaining risks, limitations, blocker codes, and recovery owner; and
+- confirmation that no catalog or external product state was changed.
+
+The only persistent outputs are user-authorized files within the coverage-product
+repository. Coverage reports remain local unless the user approves publication.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete a report or diagnosis only when its scope, command, repository state, and
+limitations make the evidence reproducible. Complete an improvement only when the
+new tests fail for the intended missing behavior where practicable, pass with the
+final implementation, the applicable coverage run succeeds, configured thresholds
+are honored, and the diff contains no unapproved files.
+
+Also require behavior-bearing assertions, no metric-only production changes, no
+weakened exclusions or thresholds, no exposed secrets, and explicit disclosure of
+flaky, skipped, platform-limited, or unmeasured paths. Human review remains required
+for consequential production changes and any decision to publish or enforce a new
+coverage gate.

+++ b/product/.agents/skills/coverage-workflow/SKILL.md
@@ -0,0 +1,147 @@
+---
+name: coverage-workflow
+description: Use when a user asks to inspect, explain, improve, or enforce automated test coverage in the coverage-product repository, including identifying meaningful gaps, adding focused tests, or assessing a coverage regression.
+---
+
+# Coverage Workflow
+
+## Overview
+
+Turn a coverage request into an evidence-backed, repository-local result. Inspect
+how coverage is measured, connect uncovered behavior to product risk, and make
+the smallest approved test or configuration change that improves confidence.
+
+Coverage percentage alone is not the goal. Do not add assertions that merely
+execute lines, weaken thresholds, exclude difficult code, or change production
+behavior solely to inflate a metric. This skill does not publish reports,
+change external CI settings, or claim unexecuted validation passed.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Before acting, load the repository policy files that govern the files in scope,
+the user's request, the current worktree state, and the relevant test and
+coverage configuration. Inspect the implementation under test, nearby tests,
+the package scripts or documented commands, and any available baseline or CI
+failure supplied by the user.
+
+Treat existing user changes as owned by the user. When the request concerns a
+regression, establish the comparison base from explicit user or repository
+context; do not guess a branch, commit, threshold, or external CI state.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require:
+
+- a repository-local coverage goal or failure to investigate; and
+- enough repository evidence to identify the affected component and supported
+  test command.
+
+Optional inputs are a coverage report, baseline revision, target file or test
+suite, required threshold, failing CI output, and permission to modify tests or
+configuration.
+
+An inspect or explain request authorizes read-only diagnosis, not edits. A fix
+or improve request authorizes changes only within the user-selected repository
+scope. A percentage target without a supported measurement command is not a
+valid basis for claiming success. Conflicting thresholds, ambiguous comparison
+bases, or requests to conceal uncovered behavior are invalid combinations.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Classify the request as inspection, regression diagnosis, test improvement,
+   or coverage-policy change. Record the requested scope and whether writes are
+   authorized.
+2. Read applicable repository instructions and inspect worktree status. Locate
+   the coverage provider, configuration, package scripts, test layout, and any
+   checked-in baseline without modifying files.
+3. Reproduce the narrowest relevant test or coverage command when local policy
+   permits. Preserve the command, exit status, and salient measurements. If no
+   report can be generated, continue only with clearly labeled static evidence.
+4. Map uncovered statements, branches, or functions to observable behavior.
+   Read the implementation and existing tests, then prioritize missing error
+   paths, boundary conditions, state transitions, and user-visible outcomes.
+5. For an authorized improvement, add or refine the smallest focused tests that
+   assert behavior. Reuse established fixtures and test conventions. Change
+   coverage configuration only when the user explicitly requested a policy
+   change and repository policy permits it.
+6. Run the focused tests first. Then run the repository-supported coverage
+   command at the narrowest scope that can verify the requested outcome. Do not
+   overwrite artifacts or snapshots outside the approved scope.
+7. Compare before and after evidence on the same command, configuration, and
+   base. Inspect the diff for production changes, brittle assertions, accidental
+   exclusions, threshold reductions, and unrelated edits.
+8. Report the behavior covered, files changed, commands and results, remaining
+   gaps, and any decision that still belongs to the user or CI owner.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+| Situation | Action |
+| --- | --- |
+| Valid report and supported command exist | Use them as the measurement source. |
+| Report is stale or configuration differs | Regenerate locally or label the result unverified. |
+| Gap represents observable behavior | Prefer a focused behavioral test. |
+| Line is unreachable or generated | Gather evidence and request an explicit exclusion or design decision. |
+| Threshold change is requested | Show impact and require explicit authorization before editing policy. |
+| Only external CI can reproduce the issue | Stop local mutation and hand off exact evidence to the CI owner. |
+| Ownership, scope, or safe command is unresolved | Default deny writes and return a blocker. |
+
+Prefer risk-reducing branch coverage over superficial line execution. Preserve
+the repository's established runner, assertion style, and ownership boundaries.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Use stable blockers in the handoff:
+
+- `coverage-command-missing`: no repository-supported measurement command;
+- `coverage-baseline-ambiguous`: the requested comparison base is unresolved;
+- `coverage-report-invalid`: the report is stale, malformed, or mismatched;
+- `coverage-scope-unresolved`: the affected component or write boundary is not
+  clear;
+- `coverage-regression-unreproduced`: available local evidence does not
+  reproduce the reported regression;
+- `coverage-validation-failed`: focused tests or coverage verification failed;
+- `coverage-policy-approval-required`: a threshold, exclusion, or CI policy
+  decision needs human approval.
+
+On failure, stop further writes, preserve the observed command and result, and
+name the recovery owner. Never lower a threshold, add an exclusion, delete a
+test, or rewrite production logic to turn a failed check into a pass.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The user retains approval for coverage thresholds, exclusions, generated-code
+policy, production refactors, broad snapshot changes, and acceptance of known
+gaps. Repository or CI owners retain external CI configuration, branch
+protection, publication, and merge decisions. Ask before running commands with
+external side effects or changing files outside the explicitly authorized
+scope.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return:
+
+- request classification, inspected scope, and measurement source;
+- baseline command and result, or the reason no valid baseline exists;
+- behavior-to-gap analysis with prioritized risks;
+- changed files and a concise explanation of each authorized change;
+- focused-test and coverage commands with exit status and comparable results;
+- remaining uncovered behavior, blockers, approvals, and recovery owner; and
+- an explicit statement when results are static, partial, or locally
+  unverifiable.
+
+Do not emit or modify a Foundry catalog entry. This bundle is owned by
+`coverage-product`, runs through Codex as `$coverage-workflow`, and remains
+`standalone-only` at `product/.agents/skills/coverage-workflow`.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete only when the bundle identity and invocation agree; repository policy
+and the working tree were respected; the requested behavior is covered or the
+diagnosis is evidence-backed; focused tests pass; comparable coverage evidence
+supports any metric claim; no threshold was weakened and no unjustified
+exclusion was added; the diff contains only authorized changes; and all
+remaining gaps, human approvals, external checks, and blockers are explicit.
+
+For read-only requests, completion means an evidence-backed diagnosis with zero
+unrequested writes. For change requests, a coverage increase without meaningful
+behavioral assertions is not completion.

@@ -0,0 +1,147 @@
+---
+name: mixed-workflow
+description: Use when a request combines a UI application with a callable workflow skill and each must be planned, built, validated, and handed off as a separate deliverable without crossing ownership boundaries.
+---
+
+# Mixed Workflow
+
+## Overview
+
+Turn an approved mixed-product request into independently callable and
+independently owned deliverables. Preserve the distinction between the UI
+application and the workflow skill from selection through handoff: each keeps
+its own contract, target surface, runtime visibility, artifacts, validation,
+and completion result.
+
+This workflow does not disguise a UI as a skill, embed a skill bundle beneath
+a UI path, publish either deliverable, or approve missing contracts on the
+operator's behalf.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Before writing, load the repository policy, selected implementation task,
+approved deliverable migration, phase approvals, artifact bindings, protected
+paths, dependency plans, and existing files at every approved target. Load the
+policy and builder instructions for each selected deliverable kind. For every
+skill deliverable, also load the applicable skill-bundle and behavior-evaluation
+contracts. For every UI deliverable, load its product requirements, design
+source or prior art, application policy, and UI builder contract when present.
+
+Treat approved contracts and bindings as authoritative. Keep supplied RED or
+other harness evidence intact, and identify which validation and review gates
+are owned by an outer lifecycle or evaluation harness.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require an implementation-ready selected task containing:
+
+- approved versioned deliverable migration and current implementation phase;
+- one explicit contract per deliverable with a stable id, kind, operation,
+  role, owner, ownership boundary, target surface, visibility, and runtime
+  targets;
+- required artifacts, exact artifact bindings, and artifact-bound phase
+  approvals;
+- guidance, evaluation plan, completion conditions, and protected paths; and
+- a complete write plan and provenance requirements for every dependency step.
+
+The UI and workflow skill must have distinct deliverable ids and non-overlapping
+target surfaces. A create contract must not collide with an existing owned
+artifact. An update contract must resolve exactly one existing identity.
+Optional inputs may include approved design assets, declared support artifacts,
+and outer-harness evidence.
+
+Reject combinations that merge deliverables into one contract, bind an
+artifact to the wrong role, place a skill bundle in the UI target, request an
+undeclared helper, leave ownership unresolved, or require writes outside the
+approved boundaries.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Inspect the selected task and enumerate its deliverable contracts without
+   changing tracker state.
+2. Partition contracts by kind and assign each to its matching builder. Record
+   the deliverable id, ownership, surface, visibility, runtime, and approved
+   artifacts before any write.
+3. Check that target paths do not overlap and that every proposed file is
+   covered by both an artifact binding and a phase approval. Default to no
+   write for any unmatched path.
+4. Build the UI only with its UI application builder. Keep application code,
+   browser assets, and UI-specific tests inside the UI contract's boundary.
+5. Build each callable workflow skill only with its skill builder. Give every
+   skill its own substantive `SKILL.md`, matching `agents/openai.yaml`, exact
+   invocation, and independently valid identity.
+6. Add support files only when the relevant contract declares them, the
+   primary deliverable directly consumes them, and their validation is defined.
+7. Reconcile declared dependencies in their approved order. Record inputs,
+   outputs, provenance, consuming role, and boundary checks without performing
+   an unapproved external or consequential action.
+8. Validate each deliverable independently against its own deterministic,
+   runtime, security, and review gates. Then check the combined diff for path,
+   ownership, visibility, identity, and catalog separation.
+9. Report a per-deliverable result. A completed deliverable does not convert a
+   pending or blocked sibling into success; hand unresolved work to its owning
+   builder or lifecycle task.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+| Condition | Route |
+| --- | --- |
+| Contract kind is `ui-application` | Use the UI builder and UI-owned surface |
+| Contract kind is a canonical, packaged, standalone, or pack-member skill | Use the skill builder and emit a complete callable bundle |
+| Target surfaces overlap or ownership is unresolved | Stop before writes |
+| Only one deliverable has valid approval | Build only that deliverable and report the other pending |
+| Support artifact is undeclared or unbound | Exclude it and report a blocker |
+| External write or publication is required but unapproved | Stop at the human boundary |
+
+When classification is ambiguous, do not infer a builder from a filename or
+move files to a convenient surface. Request corrected contracts from the task
+owner.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Stop before new writes on `migration-pending`, `contract-missing`,
+`contract-invalid`, `ownership-unresolved`, or `phase-approval-unbound`. Use
+`identity-mismatch` when metadata, folder, and invocation disagree; use
+`support-artifact-undeclared` for unapproved resources; and use
+`dependency-write-plan-missing` or `dependency-out-of-boundary` for unsafe
+dependency work.
+
+After partial construction, preserve valid in-boundary artifacts and report
+the failing deliverable separately with observed evidence. Do not weaken a
+contract, manufacture evaluation evidence, retry a failed first attempt as if
+it were fresh, or satisfy a missing UI by writing skill files into its path.
+Recovery belongs to the contract or lifecycle owner identified in the task.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The operator retains approval of contracts, ownership, phase paths, design
+direction where unspecified, consequential dependency actions, external
+writes, promotion, publication, and release. An outer harness retains any
+explicitly delegated model runs, fresh-agent grading, deterministic validation,
+and Standards-plus-Spec review. This workflow may write only approved files in
+the selected deliverables' declared boundaries.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return one record per deliverable containing its id, kind, operation, owner,
+ownership boundary, target surface, runtime visibility and targets, builder,
+status, primary and support artifact paths, validation evidence, dependency
+provenance, blocker codes, and recovery owner. Include the combined boundary
+and catalog result, protected-path result, and lifecycle handoff.
+
+The UI and every workflow skill remain separate records even when one raw
+request created them. Report actual files and evidence; narrative assertions
+do not substitute for artifacts or gates.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete the mixed workflow only when every required deliverable independently
+satisfies its contract, identities and invocations agree, all writes match
+approved bindings, optional support is declared and consumed, dependencies
+reconcile, protected paths are unchanged, runtime visibility and catalog state
+are correct, and required deterministic, model-run, security, and human-review
+gates pass.
+
+When an outer harness owns a gate, end construction after approved file writes
+and permitted local content checks, return the artifacts as ready for that
+gate, and do not claim the overall release complete.

diff --git a/.agents/skills/mixed-workflow/SKILL.md b/.agents/skills/mixed-workflow/SKILL.md
new file mode 100644

+++ b/.agents/skills/mixed-workflow/SKILL.md
@@ -0,0 +1,116 @@
+---
+name: mixed-workflow
+description: Use when an implementation-ready task mixes a callable workflow skill with UI or other product deliverables and the approved skill must be constructed without modifying sibling products.
+---
+
+# Mixed Workflow
+
+## Overview
+
+Construct the callable skill portion of an approved mixed-product task while
+preserving the ownership and file boundaries of every sibling deliverable. The
+result is a complete, independently invocable skill bundle, not a UI
+implementation, tracker update, publishing action, command wrapper, or helper.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the repository policy, the selected implementation-ready task, its
+versioned deliverable contracts, artifact bindings, phase approvals, dependency
+plans, and supplied evaluation evidence. Identify every deliverable in the task
+so that non-skill products remain visible as boundaries, then load only the
+guidance explicitly required by the selected skill contract. Treat approved
+paths, ownership, runtime visibility, runtime targets, and protected paths as
+authoritative.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require exactly one selected skill deliverable with an approved create or
+update operation, a versioned contract, one ownership boundary, one exact target
+surface, runtime visibility and targets, required artifacts, completion
+conditions, artifact-bound phase approval, and an artifact binding covering
+each intended file. Require an explicit write plan for every dependency action.
+
+Sibling UI, documentation, validator, or tracker deliverables may be present,
+but they are context and boundaries rather than inputs authorizing this skill to
+write them. Optional support resources are valid only when the contract names
+their artifact IDs, paths, purpose, and verification. Reject conflicting
+ownership, uncovered paths, pending migration, ambiguous update identities,
+missing approvals, and undeclared support.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Confirm the task is in its approved implementation phase and the skill
+   contract is ready for its declared operation.
+2. Partition all deliverables by kind, owner, target surface, visibility, and
+   artifact binding; select only the independently callable skill contract.
+3. Resolve its identity from the exact target folder. For an update, require
+   exactly one existing matching identity.
+4. Determine the ownership branch and verify the path and catalog behavior
+   agree with it.
+5. Establish the write boundary from the skill's approved artifacts and phase
+   approvals. Explicitly exclude sibling UI and product paths.
+6. Create or update the smallest complete bundle: a substantive `SKILL.md` and
+   matching `agents/openai.yaml`. Add no unapproved resources.
+7. Check that the folder name, frontmatter name, invocation, ownership,
+   visibility, runtime targets, and required semantics agree.
+8. Inspect the resulting file list and contents for boundary violations,
+   command-first behavior, thin wrappers, helper substitutes, and accidental
+   sibling-product changes.
+9. Return the bundle identity, paths, ownership, visibility, catalog result,
+   checks, blockers, and lifecycle handoff without changing task state.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+| Condition | Action |
+| --- | --- |
+| Canonical Foundry skill | Write under `.agents/skills/<slug>/`; report `canonical-active` and catalog inclusion when complete. |
+| Project-packaged skill | Write only to its approved project skill surface; report `project-local-inactive` and catalog exclusion. |
+| Standalone product skill | Use only the exact product-owned surface; report `standalone-only` and catalog exclusion. |
+| Workflow-pack member | Build one complete bundle and exact invocation for this member only. |
+| Mixed task with a non-skill sibling | Leave the sibling untouched and report it outside this workflow's ownership. |
+| Missing, ambiguous, or contradictory authority | Deny by default and write nothing. |
+
+For an update, never create a new slug to bypass a missing or ambiguous
+identity. Promotion between ownership branches requires its own approved
+contract.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Stop before writes with `migration-pending`, `contract-missing`,
+`contract-invalid`, `ownership-unresolved`, `phase-approval-unbound`, or
+`dependency-write-plan-missing` as applicable. Stop and preserve evidence if an
+approved artifact is missing or invalid, support is undeclared, identity is
+inconsistent, a sibling boundary would be crossed, or validation detects a
+helper-only, thin-wrapper, command-first, or incomplete bundle.
+
+Report the stable blocker, observed facts, affected deliverable, and recovery
+owner. Do not broaden approvals, repair lifecycle state, modify sibling
+products, substitute a different model, or reinterpret a failed first attempt
+as passing.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The user or outer lifecycle harness retains approval of contracts, ownership,
+phase transitions, dependency and external writes, product implementation,
+promotion, catalog publication, release, and consequential actions. This skill
+may modify only the selected skill's exactly approved and bound paths. It does
+not implement a UI merely because a UI deliverable shares the task.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return the selected deliverable ID and skill identity; operation; ownership
+boundary; target surface; runtime visibility and targets; primary files;
+justified support files, if any; catalog inclusion or exclusion; local content
+check results; dependency provenance; preserved evaluation evidence; blockers;
+untouched sibling surfaces; and the lifecycle owner responsible for remaining
+gates and state transitions.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Construction is complete only when both bundle files exist, identities and the
+`$mixed-workflow` invocation match, all required semantics are substantive,
+every written path is approved and bound, no undeclared support or sibling
+product file changed, and local file-content checks pass. Deterministic suites,
+fresh GPT-5.6 Sol runs, security review, Standards-plus-Spec review, catalog
+publication, and lifecycle completion remain required when assigned to the
+outer harness; never claim those gates were run locally when they were not.

+---
+name: mixed-workflow
+description: Use when a request combines a user interface with an independently callable workflow skill and the two must remain separate, explicitly owned deliverables.
+---
+
+# Mixed Workflow
+
+## Overview
+
+Turn an approved mixed-product request into a callable workflow deliverable while
+preserving the UI as a separate product deliverable. Build only the workflow
+artifacts assigned to this skill, keep their invocation independent of the UI,
+and hand off the UI contract without implementing or disguising it as a skill.
+
+This workflow does not design UI screens, write application code, publish a
+skill, change lifecycle state, or infer approvals that are absent from the
+selected task.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the repository policy, the selected implementation-ready task, its approved
+deliverable migration, phase approvals, artifact bindings, ownership boundaries,
+runtime targets, completion conditions, and protected paths. Identify every
+deliverable in the request before selecting the callable one. Load source or
+prior-art inputs only when the selected task explicitly requires them.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require:
+
+- one selected task in its approved implementation phase;
+- a versioned callable-skill contract with an exact create or update operation;
+- one ownership boundary, target surface, runtime visibility, and runtime target;
+- artifact-bound approval for `SKILL.md` and `agents/openai.yaml`;
+- a distinct contract and owner for every UI deliverable in the same request;
+- declared validation, evaluation, and completion conditions.
+
+Optional inputs are approved support artifacts and dependency outputs with exact
+bindings and provenance. Reject a request when the UI and skill share one
+deliverable identity, ownership is ambiguous, an update resolves zero or several
+existing identities, paths exceed the approved boundary, or an optional resource
+lacks approval. A UI target cannot substitute for the callable bundle, and a
+skill bundle cannot count as the UI application.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Inventory all approved deliverable contracts and classify each by kind,
+   owner, surface, visibility, and runtime target.
+2. Select only callable-skill contracts assigned to this workflow. Record UI
+   contracts as separate handoffs and make no writes to their paths.
+3. Verify that each selected skill has exact artifact bindings and phase-bound
+   approvals for both required bundle files.
+4. Resolve the identity. For create, use the approved lowercase hyphenated
+   folder name. For update, require exactly one matching existing identity.
+5. Write a substantive `SKILL.md` whose frontmatter, workflow, boundaries,
+   outputs, failures, and completion gates agree with the selected contract.
+6. Write `agents/openai.yaml` with matching display metadata and a
+   `default_prompt` that invokes the exact `$skill-name`.
+7. Check file contents locally for identity agreement, required sections,
+   independent invocation, approved paths, and absence of UI implementation.
+8. Return the callable artifact paths plus the untouched UI handoff, evidence
+   available from the current construction step, and any stable blockers to the
+   lifecycle owner.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+| Condition | Action |
+| --- | --- |
+| Callable contract and UI contract are separate and fully approved | Build only the callable bundle |
+| Several approved callable members exist | Build one complete, independently invocable bundle per member |
+| UI contract is valid but has another owner | Preserve it as an untouched handoff |
+| Ownership, approval, identity, or binding is unresolved | Stop with zero writes |
+| Request implies promotion or publishing | Require a separate explicit contract and approval |
+
+Default to no write whenever a deliverable cannot be mapped unambiguously to
+one approved owner and artifact boundary.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Use stable blockers: `contract-missing`, `contract-invalid`,
+`ownership-unresolved`, `phase-approval-unbound`, `identity-mismatch`,
+`required-artifact-missing`, `required-artifact-invalid`,
+`support-artifact-undeclared`, or `primary-deliverable-incomplete`. Report the
+observed conflict, affected deliverable, and recovery owner. Do not merge the UI
+and skill to bypass a blocker, invent a path, broaden an approval, or leave a
+helper-only, command-first, or thin-wrapper bundle.
+
+If a failure is discovered before construction, write nothing. If local checks
+find a defect in newly constructed approved files, repair only those files or
+remove the incomplete new bundle before returning blocked.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The user or lifecycle harness retains approval of deliverable contracts,
+ownership, paths, promotion, publishing, external writes, dependency actions,
+and phase transitions. The UI owner retains all UI design and implementation.
+This workflow may write only the exact approved callable-skill artifacts and may
+not mark the lifecycle task done.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return the callable skill identity, ownership boundary, target surface, runtime
+visibility, runtime targets, primary artifact paths, local content-check result,
+and catalog expectation. Also return the separate UI deliverable identity and
+surface as a handoff without claiming it was built. Include blocker codes and
+the lifecycle owner responsible for remaining validation, review, publishing,
+and phase state.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Construction is complete only when both required skill files exist at approved
+paths; folder name, frontmatter name, and `$skill-name` invocation agree; all
+required semantics are substantive; the callable workflow remains independent
+from the UI; no undeclared support or UI files were written; ownership,
+visibility, runtime targets, and catalog expectation match the contract; and
+local content checks pass.
+
+Release remains with the outer lifecycle harness and additionally requires its
+deterministic validation, isolated GPT-5.6 Sol first-attempt scenarios, security
+checks, dependency reconciliation when applicable, and Standards-plus-Spec
+review with no unresolved critical or high findings.

+---
+name: pack-intake
+description: Use when a user wants to define, clarify, or validate an implementation-ready workflow pack containing two or more independently callable skills before construction begins.
+---
+
+# Pack Intake
+
+## Overview
+
+Turn a workflow-pack request into an inspectable intake contract for independently
+callable member skills. Establish each member's job, identity, ownership, runtime
+surface, shared inputs, and approval needs without implementing, publishing, or
+promoting any bundle.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the repository policy, selected task, approved deliverable contracts, artifact
+bindings, phase approvals, dependency plans, and existing skill identities. Treat
+declared references shared by multiple members as inputs, not as substitutes for
+complete member bundles. Preserve the user's raw request alongside the normalized
+intake so later reviewers can distinguish supplied facts from inferences.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require a raw pack request and at least two proposed member jobs. For every member,
+require or resolve a unique lowercase hyphenated name, purpose, trigger, ownership
+boundary, target surface, runtime visibility, runtime targets, operation, required
+artifacts, and completion conditions. Record optional shared references with their
+owner, consumers, approved locator, and purpose.
+
+An existing identity is required for an update. A create and update for the same
+identity, overlapping ownership claims, an undeclared shared file, or a reference
+that replaces a member `SKILL.md` is invalid. Missing consequential choices remain
+unresolved; do not silently choose ownership or approval scope.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Preserve the raw request and enumerate the distinct user-visible jobs.
+2. Split jobs into members only when each can be invoked and completed on its own.
+3. Assign one exact identity and one complete bundle target to every member.
+4. Map ownership, visibility, runtime targets, operation, artifacts, and approvals
+   for each identity; reject cross-boundary paths.
+5. Inventory shared references once, name every consuming member, and confirm each
+   reference is explicitly allowed and directly useful to those members.
+6. Separate required facts from optional inputs and list unresolved decisions.
+7. Check artifact bindings and approvals against member, role, phase, and path.
+8. Emit a normalized pack intake plus a construction order that does not imply
+   authorization to write files.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- Keep jobs in one member when they share one trigger and one completion outcome.
+- Split jobs when operators must invoke them independently or they have different
+  ownership, permissions, inputs, or completion gates.
+- Reuse a reference only when its contract names every consumer and the approved
+  ownership boundary permits the shared location.
+- Default to blocked when identity, ownership, approval, or artifact binding is
+  ambiguous. Do not invent a path to make the intake appear complete.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Return `pack-members-unresolved` for fewer than two independent jobs,
+`member-identity-conflict` for duplicate or ambiguous identities,
+`ownership-unresolved` for unclear boundaries, `phase-approval-unbound` for an
+approval that does not bind the member artifact, and `shared-reference-undeclared`
+for unsupported shared material. Include observed evidence, the smallest recovery
+decision, and its owner. Stop before construction or external writes.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The user or lifecycle owner approves member boundaries, ownership, target paths,
+shared-reference creation, consequential dependencies, promotion, and publishing.
+This workflow may normalize supplied contracts and identify gaps; it does not grant
+approval, mutate trackers, create bundles, or contact external systems.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return the pack identity; one member record per independent invocation; exact
+ownership, surface, visibility, runtime, operation, artifact, and approval fields;
+a shared-reference matrix of locator, owner, purpose, and consumers; unresolved
+decisions; blocker codes; and the lifecycle owner for construction handoff. Keep
+the original request and provenance for every normalized value.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete intake only when every member is independently callable, identities and
+paths are unique, artifact bindings and approvals are exact, shared references are
+declared and consumer-bound, invalid combinations are absent, and no ownership or
+authority decision was inferred. Report readiness for construction, not completion
+of the pack itself.

+---
+name: pack-review
+description: Use when a workflow pack with multiple callable skill bundles needs an evidence-based review of member independence, shared references, boundaries, metadata, and release readiness.
+---
+
+# Pack Review
+
+## Overview
+
+Review a workflow pack as a collection of independently callable skill bundles and
+issue a release decision grounded in repository evidence. Check shared references
+without treating them as pack members. Do not repair files, approve exceptions,
+publish the pack, or replace required independent validation.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read repository policy, the selected task and approvals, every member deliverable
+contract and artifact binding, each member's complete `SKILL.md` and
+`agents/openai.yaml`, declared shared references, dependency provenance, catalog
+projection, and supplied deterministic and fresh-agent evidence. Preserve evidence
+identity, timestamps, and first-attempt status; do not convert narrative claims into
+validation results.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require a pack contract naming at least two member deliverables, exact member bundle
+paths, ownership and runtime expectations, declared shared references, and the
+release gates applicable to the current phase. Each member must contain substantive
+skill semantics and metadata that invokes its exact `$skill-name`.
+
+Accept missing gate evidence only as an explicit review blocker. Reject duplicate
+member identities, a shared reference presented as a callable member, undeclared
+support, mixed ownership without separate approvals, self-attested evaluation, and
+approval or evidence from a different deliverable, role, artifact, or phase.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Freeze the review scope from the pack contract and record the inspected paths.
+2. Match every member deliverable to exactly one bundle and one invocation identity.
+3. Inspect each bundle independently for required metadata and substantive overview,
+   context, inputs, workflow, decisions, failures, boundaries, outputs, and gates.
+4. Cross-check target surface, ownership, visibility, runtime targets, artifact
+   bindings, and phase approvals against actual files.
+5. Trace every shared reference to its declaration, owner, purpose, consumers, and
+   direct use; flag speculative, duplicated, or unreferenced support.
+6. Confirm members remain useful when invoked alone and do not depend on hidden state
+   or another member impersonating their required contract.
+7. Evaluate supplied deterministic, first-attempt model-run, dependency, security,
+   catalog, and human-review evidence without running missing gates.
+8. Classify findings by severity and return pass, blocked, or fail with precise
+   remediation ownership.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- Pass a member only when its own bundle is complete and independently invocable.
+- Allow shared references when declared, boundary-safe, directly consumed, and not
+  used to hide missing member semantics.
+- Mark absent required evidence as blocked; mark contradictory artifacts or failed
+  evidence as failed.
+- Default to fail for ownership, identity, authority, protected-path, or undeclared
+  support violations. A pack-level summary cannot override a member failure.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Use stable findings including `required-artifact-missing`,
+`required-artifact-invalid`, `identity-mismatch`, `ownership-unresolved`,
+`phase-approval-unbound`, `support-artifact-undeclared`, `unreferenced-helper`,
+`eval-evidence-missing`, `eval-evidence-failed`, `catalog-bundle-incomplete`, and
+`primary-deliverable-incomplete`. Cite the observed file or evidence, affected
+member, severity, recovery action, and recovery owner. Do not edit the reviewed
+pack or weaken a gate to obtain a pass.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The user or outer lifecycle harness owns exceptions, approvals, remediation,
+reruns, Standards-plus-Spec signoff, promotion, catalog changes, and publishing.
+This reviewer reads approved in-scope artifacts and reports findings only; it does
+not execute model runs, validators, dependencies, external writes, or phase changes.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return pack identity and scope; a per-member verdict with identity, invocation,
+bundle path, ownership, visibility, and semantic checks; a shared-reference usage
+matrix; catalog and boundary results; supplied gate-evidence status; ordered
+findings with severity and blocker code; an overall pass, blocked, or fail result;
+and the lifecycle owner responsible for the next action.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete the review only after every in-scope member and declared shared reference
+has an evidence-backed disposition. Release readiness requires all bundles valid,
+independent invocation proven, boundaries and catalog projection correct, required
+deterministic and first-attempt model evidence passing, dependencies reconciled,
+security gates satisfied, and human review free of unresolved critical or high
+findings. Otherwise return blocked or fail without publishing.

+---
+name: pack-intake
+description: Use when a user proposes a workflow-pack member or pack change and the request must be normalized into an implementation-ready intake contract before construction or review.
+---
+
+# Pack Intake
+
+## Overview
+
+Turn a workflow-pack request into a precise, inspectable intake result. Identify
+each independently callable member, its ownership, target, runtime visibility,
+artifacts, approvals, dependencies, and acceptance conditions. Do not implement,
+publish, promote, review, or silently repair the request.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the repository policy governing the requested target, the selected task and
+its current phase, existing deliverable contracts and artifact bindings, and any
+named source requirements or prior skill identities. For an update, inspect the
+candidate skill locations needed to resolve one exact identity. Treat explicit
+operator approvals and protected-path rules as authoritative.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require a requested outcome and enough repository context to identify the owning
+project. Accept optional proposed names, target surfaces, runtime targets,
+dependencies, support resources, and approval evidence. A workflow pack must
+name or permit identification of every independently callable member.
+
+Reject conflicting ownership and target claims, a create/update ambiguity,
+multiple skills presented as one callable member, approvals not bound to the
+deliverable and artifact role, or dependency work without an explicit write
+plan. Never infer permission to publish, promote, write externally, or modify a
+protected path.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Restate the requested user outcome and separate intake work from construction.
+2. Classify the result as a single skill, a workflow pack of independent members,
+   or an unresolved request.
+3. Assign each member a lowercase hyphenated identity and a create or update
+   operation; for updates, resolve exactly one existing identity.
+4. Determine one ownership boundary, exact target surface, runtime visibility,
+   runtime targets, and owner project for every member.
+5. Enumerate the primary `SKILL.md` and `agents/openai.yaml` bundle. Include a
+   support resource only when its purpose, consumer, approval, and verification
+   are explicit.
+6. Bind each artifact to its deliverable, role, locator, implementation phase,
+   and approval. Record protected paths and permitted write prefixes.
+7. Capture required guidance, scenario-based evaluation needs, completion
+   conditions, and a write plan for every dependency step.
+8. Report an implementation-ready intake contract or stop with stable blockers
+   and the facts the operator must supply.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- Use canonical ownership only for a repository-approved active Foundry target.
+- Use project-packaged ownership for a project-local, inactive bundle.
+- Use standalone ownership only when exact product policy owns the target.
+- Model a workflow pack as one pack contract plus one complete, independently
+  invocable contract per member.
+- Default to unresolved when policy, ownership, identity, approval, or boundary
+  evidence does not select exactly one valid branch.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Stop without artifact writes on `contract-missing`, `contract-invalid`,
+`ownership-unresolved`, `phase-approval-unbound`,
+`dependency-write-plan-missing`, or ambiguous update identity. Report the
+observed conflict, affected member, permitted recovery input, and the operator
+or lifecycle owner responsible. Preserve explicit user choices; do not weaken
+requirements to make intake appear complete.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The operator retains approval of identity, ownership, target, artifact roles,
+external or dependency writes, promotion, publication, and consequential
+actions. The lifecycle owner controls phase transitions and tracker mutation.
+This skill produces intake state only and performs no implementation writes.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return the request classification; pack and member deliverable identities;
+operation, role, ownership, owner, target, visibility, and runtime targets;
+required and allowed artifacts; artifact bindings and approvals; dependency
+plans; evaluation requirements; completion conditions; protected boundaries;
+blockers; and the lifecycle handoff. Make unresolved fields explicit rather
+than filling them with guesses.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Intake is ready only when every member is independently callable, all identities
+and targets agree, every required artifact is bound to an approval, optional
+support is justified, dependencies have bounded write plans, acceptance checks
+are inspectable, and no ownership or authority question remains. Human review
+must confirm the contract reflects the request before construction begins.

@@ -0,0 +1,99 @@
+---
+name: pack-review
+description: Use when completed workflow-pack skill bundles must be reviewed against their approved contracts, ownership boundaries, invocation metadata, and release gates.
+---
+
+# Pack Review
+
+## Overview
+
+Review an implemented workflow pack and each member as an independently callable
+skill. Compare actual files and evidence with the approved contract, then return
+findings and a release recommendation. Do not implement fixes, rewrite evidence,
+publish, promote, mutate tracker state, or approve on the operator's behalf.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read repository and target policy, the approved pack and member deliverable
+contracts, artifact bindings and phase approvals, the complete emitted bundles,
+declared support resources, protected-path rules, dependency outcomes, and the
+required deterministic, fresh-agent, security, and human-review evidence. Use
+the preserved first-attempt evidence rather than a later narrative summary.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require at least one approved member contract, its exact target bundle, and the
+evidence required by its completion conditions. For a pack, require the pack's
+member list and one contract per member. Accept optional declared support files
+and dependency results only when bound to a member and artifact role.
+
+Treat missing artifacts, mismatched identities, undeclared resources, absent
+first-attempt evidence, self-attested validation, and incomplete dependency
+provenance as failures—not optional omissions. Do not combine evidence from
+isolated scenario runs or accept retries as first attempts.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Inventory only contract-bound files and map each file to a member, artifact,
+   role, approval, and permitted target prefix.
+2. Confirm each member contains a substantive `SKILL.md` and matching
+   `agents/openai.yaml`, with exact folder, frontmatter, and `$skill-name`
+   invocation identity.
+3. Inspect every skill for overview, required context, input rules, imperative
+   workflow, decisions, failures, human boundaries, output, and completion gates.
+4. Verify ownership, target surface, runtime visibility, runtime targets, and
+   catalog treatment against the approved branch.
+5. Reject command-first, helper-only, thin-wrapper, code-only, duplicate,
+   speculative, or unreferenced resources.
+6. Compare protected paths and the actual diff with approvals. Reconcile each
+   dependency output, role, boundary, and provenance record.
+7. Check deterministic results and isolated GPT-5.6 Sol first-attempt scenario
+   evidence, plus security and Standards-and-Spec review findings.
+8. Emit severity-ranked findings with evidence and recovery ownership, then
+   recommend release only if every member and the pack-level conditions pass.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- Review canonical members for complete active-catalog eligibility.
+- Review project-packaged and standalone members as inactive outside the
+  Foundry catalog and under their exact owner policy.
+- Fail the whole pack when any required member is missing, invalid, duplicated,
+  or not independently invocable.
+- Mark a gate blocked when evidence is unavailable; absence is never a pass.
+- Default deny release for boundary ambiguity, conflicting approvals, or any
+  unresolved critical or high finding.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Use stable codes such as `required-artifact-missing`,
+`required-artifact-invalid`, `identity-mismatch`, `incomplete-metadata`,
+`support-artifact-undeclared`, `eval-evidence-missing`,
+`eval-evidence-failed`, `dependency-provenance-missing`,
+`dependency-out-of-boundary`, `catalog-bundle-incomplete`, `thin-wrapper`, and
+`command-first`. For each finding, identify severity, observed evidence,
+affected member, violated contract, required recovery, and recovery owner. Stop
+the release recommendation on critical or high findings without changing files.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The operator or designated reviewer owns exception acceptance, consequential
+dependency approval, promotion, publication, and final release. The lifecycle
+owner controls task state and phase transitions. This skill is read-only and
+must not fix artifacts or manufacture missing review evidence.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return the reviewed pack and member identities; inspected primary and support
+files; ownership, visibility, catalog, boundary, and dependency results;
+deterministic and model-run evidence disposition; findings grouped by severity
+and stable code; release recommendation; blockers; recovery owners; and the
+lifecycle handoff. Distinguish observed facts from reviewer inference.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Recommend release only when all required files exist, identities and metadata
+agree, semantics are substantive, writes remain inside approved boundaries,
+support and dependencies reconcile, catalog treatment is correct, deterministic
+checks pass, every required GPT-5.6 Sol scenario passed on its preserved first
+attempt, security checks pass, and Standards-and-Spec review has no unresolved
+critical or high finding.

+---
+name: pack-intake
+description: Use when a workflow-pack request must be converted into an implementation-ready intake record with independently callable member boundaries, shared-reference ownership, approvals, and validation requirements.
+---
+
+# Pack Intake
+
+## Overview
+
+Turn a workflow-pack request into a precise, reviewable intake record before
+implementation. Identify each independently callable member, its ownership and
+files, and any genuinely shared reference. Do not implement skills, approve
+consequential actions, publish a pack, or treat shared material as a substitute
+for a member's own callable contract.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the repository policy and the selected task or request. Load any supplied
+product policy, existing skill identities, artifact inventory, runtime catalog,
+and prior decision or approval records that directly constrain the pack. Treat
+missing sources as missing; do not infer their contents or fetch external data
+without authorization.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require a pack goal, intended users, at least one requested capability, an
+owner or ownership boundary, target runtime, and target surface. For every
+candidate member, require a distinct user-visible job and an independent
+invocation. For every proposed shared reference, require a named consumer and
+a reason it must be shared.
+
+Optional inputs are existing member slugs, exact approved paths, dependencies,
+visibility, validation scenarios, protected paths, and approval evidence.
+Reject conflicting owners or paths, an update with zero or multiple matching
+identities, members that only wrap another member, shared files with no direct
+consumer, or a request that combines intake with unapproved implementation or
+publication.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Restate the pack goal, user, non-goals, evidence sources, and unresolved
+   facts without expanding scope.
+2. Split the goal into the smallest set of distinct user-visible jobs. Give
+   each proposed member one stable lowercase hyphenated identity and one
+   operator invocation in the form `$member-name`.
+3. For each member, record its trigger, required inputs, output, owner, target
+   surface, runtime visibility, operation, dependencies, and completion gates.
+4. Test independence: each member must be useful when invoked alone and must
+   own a complete `SKILL.md` and matching `agents/openai.yaml`. Reclassify
+   sequencing notes as handoffs, not hidden coupling.
+5. Inventory shared knowledge. Keep a shared reference only when two or more
+   declared members consume the same stable detail; name its exact path,
+   consumers, owner, approval, and verification. Otherwise assign the material
+   to one member or leave it out.
+6. Map every proposed file to exactly one approved artifact and ownership
+   boundary. Flag external writes, protected paths, dependency actions, and
+   catalog changes for explicit approval.
+7. Define acceptance scenarios for independent invocation, invalid input,
+   ownership boundaries, shared-reference consumption, and pack-level
+   coexistence without duplicate identities.
+8. Emit the intake record and route unresolved decisions to their owners. Do
+   not create implementation files.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- Use separate members when capabilities have different triggers, inputs, or
+  outputs and each is valuable alone.
+- Keep one member when the proposed split is merely an internal step with no
+  independent user outcome.
+- Declare a shared reference only when multiple members directly consume the
+  same stable content and the repository permits the exact path.
+- Treat canonical, project-packaged, and standalone ownership as distinct;
+  never use one path as a shortcut for another.
+- Default to unresolved and zero implementation writes when identity,
+  ownership, approval, or boundary evidence is incomplete or contradictory.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Return `intake-input-missing` for absent required inputs,
+`member-boundary-ambiguous` for overlapping or non-independent jobs,
+`identity-conflict` for unresolved create/update identity,
+`ownership-unresolved` for unclear ownership or target surface,
+`shared-reference-unjustified` for an unowned or unconsumed shared file, and
+`approval-required` for unapproved consequential actions. Preserve the facts
+and conflicts observed, name the recovery owner, and stop before file writes or
+external actions.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The user or lifecycle owner approves scope, member boundaries, product
+ownership, target paths, external writes, dependency changes, promotion,
+publication, and destructive actions. This skill may organize supplied facts
+and propose a contract; it does not grant those approvals or mutate tracker,
+catalog, repository, or external state.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return a structured intake containing pack identity and goal; one member entry
+per independent invocation; ownership, operation, target surface, visibility,
+runtime, required files, inputs, outputs, and gates for each member; shared
+references with exact consumers and approvals; dependencies and protected
+paths; acceptance scenarios; unresolved decisions with recovery owners; and a
+handoff status of `ready-for-review` or `blocked`. Clearly separate supplied
+facts, derived proposals, and missing evidence.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete only when every member is independently callable, identities are
+unique, create/update intent is unambiguous, all proposed files have owners and
+allowed paths, every shared reference has multiple named consumers, approvals
+and human boundaries are explicit, and deterministic checks can verify the
+record. Required fresh-run evaluation, security review, and human approval must
+pass in the owning lifecycle before implementation or release.

+++ b/.agents/skills/pack-review/SKILL.md
@@ -0,0 +1,112 @@
+---
+name: pack-review
+description: Use when a proposed or implemented workflow pack must be independently reviewed for callable member completeness, shared-reference integrity, ownership boundaries, and release readiness.
+---
+
+# Pack Review
+
+## Overview
+
+Review a workflow pack as evidence, not as a narrative claim. Verify every
+member can be invoked and understood independently, while shared references
+remain declared support with clear consumers. Do not repair artifacts, approve
+release, publish, or silently reinterpret missing evidence as a pass.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the applicable repository and product policies, approved pack and member
+contracts, artifact bindings, approval records, protected paths, actual file
+tree and diff, runtime or catalog projection, validation evidence, dependency
+provenance, and prior findings. When reviewing an intake-only proposal, require
+the complete intake record and label implementation-only checks not observed.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require the pack identity, all declared member identities, ownership boundary,
+target surfaces, runtime visibility, required artifacts, and the evidence to be
+reviewed. Each member must provide an exact invocation, trigger, inputs,
+workflow, outputs, failure behavior, human boundaries, and completion gates.
+
+Optional inputs include shared references, dependencies, scenario results,
+catalog output, security findings, and an implementation diff. Reject a review
+request that omits a declared member, mixes incompatible contract versions,
+uses self-attestation as independent evidence, supplies retries as first-run
+results, or asks this skill to modify the subject while judging it.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Freeze the review subject by recording contract versions, supplied files,
+   evidence identities, and the exact diff or proposal snapshot.
+2. Reconcile the pack member list against contracts and artifacts. Report
+   missing, extra, or duplicate identities before semantic review.
+3. Inspect each member separately. Confirm folder, frontmatter name, invocation
+   in `agents/openai.yaml`, trigger, required context, input contract, workflow,
+   decisions, failures, human boundaries, output contract, and completion gate
+   agree and are substantive.
+4. Test independent callability using only that member's declared contract.
+   Fail members that are thin wrappers, command-first entry points, or dependent
+   on unstated execution of another member.
+5. Review each shared reference for exact approval, owner, path, named direct
+   consumers, policy allowance, and verification. Confirm members remain
+   complete contracts rather than pointers to shared content.
+6. Check ownership, target paths, runtime visibility, catalog membership,
+   protected paths, external writes, and dependency provenance against the
+   approved boundaries.
+7. Reconcile deterministic, fresh-run, security, and human-review evidence.
+   Preserve first-attempt failures and distinguish `failed`, `blocked`, and
+   `not-observed` from `passed`.
+8. Assign severity and a stable finding code to every gap, identify its owner
+   and required evidence, then issue the pack verdict without editing files.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- Pass a member only when its own complete bundle and independent invocation
+  satisfy its approved contract.
+- Accept a shared reference only when it is approved, directly consumed, and
+  verified; an undeclared or unused shared file is a finding.
+- Mark implementation checks `not-observed` for an intake-only proposal; do not
+  convert absence into failure unless implementation evidence was required.
+- Block readiness for any critical or high finding, failed required check,
+  ownership breach, missing primary artifact, or missing required evidence.
+- Default to `blocked` when the subject, evidence identity, or authority is
+  ambiguous.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Use `review-input-missing` for absent review context,
+`member-missing` or `member-extra` for pack reconciliation,
+`identity-mismatch` for bundle disagreement,
+`member-not-independent` for wrappers or hidden coupling,
+`shared-reference-invalid` for undeclared, unowned, or unconsumed support,
+`boundary-violation` for unauthorized paths or actions,
+`evidence-invalid` for decorative or misidentified evidence, and
+`release-gate-failed` when a required gate does not pass. Return observed
+evidence, severity, recovery owner, and the exact rerun or approval needed;
+never patch the reviewed subject during the review.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+Humans retain approval of contracts, risk acceptance, ownership, external or
+dependency writes, promotion, publication, and release. The lifecycle harness
+owns model runs and phase transitions. This skill may inspect and report only;
+it cannot waive findings, manufacture evidence, or mark the lifecycle task
+done.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return the frozen subject identity; per-member bundle and independence results;
+shared-reference findings; ownership, visibility, catalog, boundary, security,
+dependency, and evidence results; each finding's code, severity, evidence,
+recovery owner, and required action; and one verdict: `ready`, `not-ready`, or
+`blocked`. Include `not-observed` checks explicitly and keep pack-level results
+separate from member-level results.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+The review is complete only when every declared member and artifact has been
+reconciled, all applicable checks have evidence, finding severities and owners
+are recorded, and the verdict follows the decision rules. A `ready` verdict
+requires complete independent bundles, valid shared support, correct ownership
+and visibility, passing deterministic and required fresh-run checks, security
+clearance, dependency reconciliation, and human review with no unresolved
+critical or high findings.

@@ -0,0 +1,116 @@
+---
+name: dependency-backed-skill
+description: Use when an approved fixture question must be answered with traceable evidence from the completed research-fixture dependency artifact.
+---
+
+# Dependency-Backed Skill
+
+## Overview
+
+Turn the approved research dependency into concise, traceable evidence for a
+fixture question. Ground every factual claim in the dependency artifact and
+separate what the artifact supports from what remains unknown. Do not perform
+new research, modify dependency outputs, promote support evidence to a primary
+deliverable, or write outside the caller's separately approved paths.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Load the governing repository policy and the selected implementation-ready task.
+From that task, identify the selected deliverable, its artifact binding, and the
+completed dependency step `eval-research-step`. Then read the declared support
+artifact `artifacts/research-evidence.md` and its provenance record. Treat the
+task's protected paths, write plan, source, role, promotion state, and recorded
+SHA-256 value as authoritative. Do not substitute prior knowledge or another
+research source.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require:
+
+- one non-empty fixture question;
+- the selected task containing dependency step `eval-research-step` in `done`
+  state;
+- the readable artifact `artifacts/research-evidence.md` bound to the selected
+  deliverable with role `support` and promotion state `support-only`;
+- provenance naming the source and `content_sha256`.
+
+An optional output shape may request prose or a claim/evidence table. It may not
+override grounding, provenance, or boundary requirements. Reject multiple
+questions when their evidence cannot be assessed independently, a replacement
+artifact path, a missing or placeholder digest, mismatched artifact roles, or a
+request to supplement the fixture with outside research.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Extract the exact fixture question without broadening its scope.
+2. Reconcile the dependency step, artifact binding, artifact role, promotion
+   state, expected path, and provenance as one consistent chain.
+3. Compute the artifact's SHA-256 digest locally and require it to equal the
+   recorded `content_sha256`; never repair a mismatch in place.
+4. Read the artifact and inventory the passages that directly bear on the
+   question, preserving distinctions, qualifications, and uncertainty.
+5. Draft the smallest answer supported by those passages. For each material
+   claim, attach a pinpoint reference using a heading and line number or another
+   stable location available in the artifact.
+6. Label interpretations as inferences and state why the cited evidence permits
+   them. Record unsupported parts of the question as evidence gaps rather than
+   guessing.
+7. Return the answer, claim-level evidence, provenance, limitations, and
+   dependency reconciliation. Make no file or tracker writes.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+| Condition | Action |
+| --- | --- |
+| Provenance, binding, digest, and role agree | Produce grounded evidence. |
+| Evidence supports only part of the question | Answer that part and report the gap. |
+| Passages conflict | Present the conflict; do not silently choose a side. |
+| Artifact is absent, unreadable, stale, or mismatched | Stop with the corresponding blocker. |
+| Request requires new research or writes | Stop and return the required approval or owner. |
+
+Default deny applies whenever dependency identity or authority is ambiguous.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Stop without an answer when the dependency output is missing
+(`dependency-output-missing`), provenance is absent or has a placeholder digest
+(`dependency-provenance-missing`), the binding or support role differs
+(`dependency-role-mismatch`), or the path escapes the declared write plan
+(`dependency-out-of-boundary`). Use `dependency-integrity-mismatch` when the
+computed digest differs from provenance and `evidence-insufficient` when no
+passage supports the question. Report the observed facts and name the selected
+task or dependency owner as recovery owner; never edit protected paths or
+invent replacement evidence.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The user or outer harness retains approval for changing the question, rerunning
+research, accepting a changed digest, promoting the support artifact, editing
+the task, publishing, or making external writes. This skill only reads the
+approved dependency and returns an evidence-backed response.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return:
+
+- `question`: the exact fixture question;
+- `answer`: only supported conclusions;
+- `evidence`: claim, artifact path, and pinpoint location for each material
+  conclusion;
+- `inferences` and `evidence_gaps`, each explicit even when empty;
+- `provenance`: dependency step, selected skill, source, path, recorded digest,
+  computed digest, artifact role, and promotion state;
+- `dependency_status`: `reconciled` or a stable blocker code;
+- `writes_performed`: always an empty list.
+
+The handoff remains with the invoking lifecycle task; this skill does not change
+task phase or status.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete only when identity, binding, role, path, promotion state, and digest
+all reconcile; every material claim has a pinpoint citation; inferences and gaps
+are explicit; no outside knowledge is presented as evidence; no protected or
+external write occurred; and the requested output fields are present. Model-run,
+deterministic, security, and Standards-plus-Spec gates remain owned by the outer
+harness and must pass before release.

@@ -0,0 +1,115 @@
+---
+name: dependency-backed-skill
+description: Use when an implementation-ready workflow deliverable must verify and report approved dependency evidence, provenance, role, and write-boundary compliance before handoff.
+---
+
+# Dependency-Backed Skill
+
+## Overview
+
+Reconcile a deliverable with evidence produced by an approved dependency step.
+Confirm that the expected output exists, is bound to the supported deliverable,
+has the declared support role and promotion state, and carries the required
+provenance. Produce an evidence-based readiness result without running the
+dependency, changing its output, promoting support into a primary artifact, or
+advancing lifecycle state.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the repository policy and the selected implementation-ready task. From the
+task, load the selected deliverable contract, artifact bindings, phase approval,
+dependency step, dependency write plan, dependency artifact record, and
+dependency provenance record. Inspect only the dependency output paths needed
+to answer the request and only when repository policy permits them. Treat task
+contracts as authoritative; do not infer missing approvals or provenance from a
+narrative claim.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require exactly one selected deliverable and one or more dependency steps whose
+`supported_deliverable_id` matches it. Each step must declare its capability,
+dependency project, selected skill, purpose, expected inputs and outputs,
+protected paths, provenance requirements, complete write plan, and terminal
+status. Each output must have a matching dependency artifact and provenance
+record with the same artifact id, step id, deliverable id, path, artifact role,
+and promotion state.
+
+Accept an optional request to check a subset of dependency artifacts, provided
+all artifacts required by the selected deliverable are still evaluated before
+readiness is reported. Reject mixed deliverable ids, duplicate artifact ids,
+conflicting paths, missing write zones, external writes without explicit
+approval, or a support artifact presented as a primary deliverable.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Resolve the selected deliverable and its exact dependency steps. Stop if the
+   relationship is absent or ambiguous.
+2. For each step, compare every expected output path with its allowed write
+   zones and expected output paths. Confirm that no declared output targets a
+   protected path or an unapproved external location.
+3. Match each expected output to exactly one dependency artifact and exactly
+   one provenance record. Compare artifact id, step id, deliverable id, path,
+   role, and promotion state field by field.
+4. Confirm the dependency step is complete and the output exists at the exact
+   declared path. Do not create, repair, normalize, or relocate the output.
+5. Verify every required provenance field. For content hashes, compute the
+   digest from the existing file using the declared algorithm and compare it
+   with the recorded value; a placeholder or self-attestation is not evidence.
+6. Inspect the output only as needed to confirm it satisfies the dependency's
+   expected-output purpose. Keep its role `support` and its promotion state
+   `support-only` unless a separate approved promotion contract exists.
+7. Emit one reconciliation result per dependency artifact, followed by an
+   aggregate ready or blocked result and the lifecycle owner responsible for
+   recovery or handoff.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+An exact, in-boundary, present output with matching records and verified
+provenance passes reconciliation. A completed step with a missing output is
+blocked, not silently rerun. A path outside an allowed write zone, inside a
+protected path, or external without approval is a boundary failure. Conflicting
+records are invalid even when the file exists. Support evidence may inform the
+deliverable but never substitutes for its primary skill bundle.
+
+Default deny whenever ownership, approval, identity, path containment, role,
+promotion state, or provenance cannot be established from the selected task
+and observed repository content.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Return stable blockers: `dependency-write-plan-missing` for an incomplete plan;
+`dependency-output-missing` for an absent expected file;
+`dependency-provenance-missing` for absent, placeholder, or unverifiable
+provenance; `dependency-role-mismatch` for inconsistent role or promotion
+state; and `dependency-out-of-boundary` for a forbidden path or write. Include
+the affected step, artifact, observed evidence, and recovery owner. Stop without
+writes on any blocker, and never rerun a dependency or weaken its contract to
+manufacture a pass.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The operator or outer lifecycle owns dependency execution, external writes,
+contract changes, provenance repair, artifact promotion, publishing, and phase
+transitions. This skill performs read-only reconciliation. It must not modify
+dependency outputs, task state, protected paths, primary deliverables, or
+external systems.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return the selected deliverable id and ownership; each dependency step and
+selected skill; each artifact id, exact path, role, and promotion state; write
+zone and protected-path findings; required and observed provenance including
+the independently computed content digest; purpose-satisfaction evidence; and
+an aggregate `ready` or `blocked` result with stable blocker codes. Distinguish
+observed facts from conclusions and identify the lifecycle task that owns the
+handoff. Do not emit or rewrite evidence files.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete only when every expected dependency output is present, uniquely
+reconciled, in boundary, purpose-appropriate, support-role consistent, and
+backed by verified provenance; the primary deliverable remains independently
+complete; deterministic dependency and boundary checks pass; required
+GPT-5.6 Sol first-attempt evaluation evidence is supplied by the outer harness;
+security checks pass; and Standards-plus-Spec review has no unresolved critical
+or high finding. Otherwise return blocked with no state transition.
