@@ -0,0 +1,113 @@
+---
+name: dependency-backed-skill
+description: Gamitin kapag kailangang sagutin ang fixture question mula sa approved research evidence habang pinapanatili at nire-report ang source at content SHA-256 provenance.
+---
+
+# Dependency-Backed Skill
+
+## Overview
+
+Gumawa ng evidence-backed na sagot sa fixture question gamit lamang ang
+approved dependency artifact na `artifacts/research-evidence.md`. Panatilihing
+nakakabit sa bawat output ang provenance ng evidence. Hindi nito nire-refresh,
+pinapalitan, o pine-promote ang support artifact, at hindi ito nagsasagawa ng
+external research o writes.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Basahin ang repository policy at ang selected implementation-ready task bago
+gamitin ang evidence. Mula sa task contract, i-resolve ang dependency step
+`eval-research-step`, artifact `research-evidence`, at ang provenance record
+para sa `artifacts/research-evidence.md`. Basahin ang artifact content lamang
+kapag ang path, role, promotion state, source, at digest record ay tumutugma sa
+approved contract.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Required ang:
+
+- isang `fixture question` na masasagot mula sa approved evidence;
+- readable na `artifacts/research-evidence.md`;
+- provenance na may `source` at `content_sha256` para sa parehong artifact;
+- `artifact_role: support` at `promotion_state: support-only`.
+
+Optional ang requested response format o haba, basta hindi nito hinihinging
+mag-imbento ng claim. Invalid ang ibang evidence path, missing o blank na
+provenance field, mismatched artifact/step/deliverable identity, primary-role
+promotion, external lookup, o request na baguhin ang dependency artifact.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. I-validate na ang question at dependency record ay nakatali sa
+   `eval-dependency-backed-1` at `eval-research-step`.
+2. I-confirm na ang exact evidence path ay `artifacts/research-evidence.md`, ang
+   role ay `support`, at ang promotion state ay `support-only`.
+3. I-confirm na present ang provenance `source` at `content_sha256`. Kung ang
+   recorded digest ay placeholder o hindi reconciled sa supplied artifact,
+   huminto; huwag mag-claim ng verified provenance.
+4. Basahin ang evidence at piliin lamang ang passages o facts na direktang
+   sumasagot sa fixture question. Ihiwalay ang direct evidence sa inference.
+5. Bumuo ng concise answer. Markahan bilang inference ang anumang synthesis at
+   huwag lumampas sa suportado ng artifact.
+6. Maglabas ng provenance block na naglalaman ng artifact id, exact path,
+   dependency project, selected skill, source, content SHA-256, role, at
+   promotion state.
+7. I-report ang anumang unsupported portion ng question sa halip na hulaan o
+   gumamit ng undeclared source.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- Kapag valid at reconciled ang evidence at provenance, sagutin ang supported
+  scope at isama ang provenance block.
+- Kapag partial lamang ang coverage, ibigay ang supported portion at ilista ang
+  evidence gap.
+- Kapag missing ang artifact, gamitin ang blocker
+  `dependency-output-missing` at huminto nang walang write.
+- Kapag missing o placeholder/unreconciled ang required provenance, gamitin ang
+  `dependency-provenance-missing` at huminto nang walang write.
+- Kapag mali ang role o promotion state, gamitin ang
+  `dependency-role-mismatch`. Kapag nasa labas ng approved path o nangangailangan
+  ng external write, gamitin ang `dependency-out-of-boundary`.
+
+Default-deny ang lahat ng source at action na hindi tahasang approved.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Sa failure, ibalik ang stable blocker code, ang observed mismatch o missing
+field, at ang recovery owner: ang lifecycle task o dependency producer. Huwag
+baguhin ang artifact, provenance record, digest, contract, o protected path para
+magmukhang valid. Huwag mag-fallback sa web, ibang skill, o sariling kaalaman.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+Ang operator o outer harness ang nag-aapprove ng dependency execution,
+artifact promotion, external writes, provenance replacement, publishing, at
+contract changes. Read-only consumption at evidence-backed synthesis lamang ang
+saklaw ng skill na ito. Ang `eval-task.json`, builder bundle, at dependency
+artifact ay hindi dapat baguhin.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Magbalik ng:
+
+1. `answer`: ang supported na sagot sa fixture question;
+2. `evidence_basis`: claims o artifact sections na ginamit, kasama ang malinaw
+   na inference labels kung mayroon;
+3. `provenance`: `artifact_id`, `step_id`, `dependency_project`,
+   `selected_skill`, `source`, `path`, `content_sha256`, `artifact_role`, at
+   `promotion_state`;
+4. `gaps`: unsupported portions, o empty list;
+5. `blockers`: stable blocker codes, o empty list.
+
+Walang file write ang normal output. Ang handoff owner ay ang invoking
+lifecycle task.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete lamang kapag exact ang bundle identity, directly supported ng
+approved artifact ang sagot, reconciled at kumpleto ang source-plus-digest
+provenance, preserved ang support-only role, walang undeclared source o write,
+at walang unresolved blocker. Ang outer harness ang may-ari ng deterministic
+validation, GPT-5.6 Sol fresh-agent evaluation, security checks, at
+Standards-plus-Spec review; hindi inaangkin ng skill na ito na pasado ang mga
+gate na iyon nang walang kanilang evidence.

+---
+name: mixed-workflow
+description: Use when an approved implementation task combines a callable workflow skill with a separate UI application deliverable and each product must remain in its own ownership boundary.
+---
+
+# Mixed Workflow
+
+## Overview
+
+Construct the approved callable workflow bundle in its exact canonical surface
+while preserving a separate UI application as an independently owned
+deliverable. Keep identities, files, visibility, and evidence distinct. This
+skill does not invent deliverables, merge product surfaces, publish catalog
+state, or perform unapproved dependency and external writes.
+
+## Required Context
+
+Read the repository policy, selected implementation-ready task, approved
+deliverable contracts, artifact bindings, phase approvals, dependency write
+plans, and protected paths. For every selected product, identify its owner,
+target surface, runtime visibility, runtime targets, required artifacts,
+allowed support, completion conditions, and prior evidence. Load product policy
+or prior art only when the task requires it and permits access.
+
+## Input Contract
+
+Require an implementation-phase task with an approved versioned migration and
+at least two separately identified contracts: one callable skill and one UI
+application. Each artifact must have a deliverable id, artifact id and role,
+exact locator, matching phase approval, operation, ownership boundary, runtime
+visibility, runtime targets, and completion condition. An update must resolve
+one existing identity. Dependencies require complete write plans.
+
+Optional inputs are declared support artifacts, product policy, prior-art
+references, and pre-existing evaluation evidence. Reject missing or conflicting
+bindings, mixed ownership for one artifact, undeclared support, ambiguous update
+identities, unsafe dependencies, and requests to place UI implementation inside
+the callable skill bundle.
+
+## Workflow
+
+1. Validate the selected phase, migration, contracts, approvals, and bindings
+   before writing any file.
+2. Partition artifacts by deliverable id and ownership boundary; record the
+   callable skill and UI application as separate products.
+3. Resolve create or update identity for each product and verify every proposed
+   path is covered by both its binding and phase approval.
+4. Construct the callable skill as a substantive `SKILL.md` with matching
+   `agents/openai.yaml`; make the folder, frontmatter name, and `$name`
+   invocation agree.
+5. Construct only the UI artifacts explicitly required by its own contract in
+   the UI-owned surface. Do not use the callable bundle as a container for UI
+   files or claim an unapproved UI implementation exists.
+6. Add support files only when their artifact ids, purpose, consumer, approval,
+   and verification are declared.
+7. Inspect the resulting file set, identities, metadata, ownership boundaries,
+   visibility, and protected-path diff. Reconcile declared dependency outputs
+   and provenance when dependencies exist.
+8. Return separate artifact and evidence records for the callable skill and UI
+   application to the lifecycle owner; leave phase changes and publishing to
+   that owner.
+
+## Decision Points
+
+- For canonical ownership, write only `.agents/skills/<slug>/`, require
+  `canonical-active`, and allow catalog inclusion only after the bundle is
+  complete.
+- For project-packaged ownership, use only the approved project-local surface,
+  require `project-local-inactive`, and exclude it from the Foundry catalog.
+- For standalone ownership, use the exact product-owned surface, require
+  `standalone-only`, and exclude it from the Foundry catalog.
+- For updates, proceed only when exactly one identity resolves; creation under
+  a new slug is not an update fallback.
+- If the UI contract authorizes only a bounded artifact set, emit only that set
+  and report its actual scope. Never fill perceived gaps with undeclared files.
+- If any contract, ownership, approval, or binding is unresolved, default to no
+  writes.
+
+## Failure Handling
+
+Stop before writes with `migration-pending`, `contract-missing`,
+`contract-invalid`, `ownership-unresolved`, `phase-approval-unbound`, or
+`dependency-write-plan-missing` as applicable. Stop and preserve observed
+evidence for `identity-mismatch`, `required-artifact-missing`,
+`required-artifact-invalid`, `support-artifact-undeclared`, boundary violations,
+incomplete metadata, or incomplete primary deliverables. Recovery belongs to
+the task owner for contract and approval defects, the product owner for product
+policy or identity defects, and the dependency owner for dependency defects.
+Never weaken a contract, move one product into the other's boundary, or rewrite
+a failed first attempt as a pass.
+
+## Human Boundaries
+
+The operator or outer lifecycle owns contract approval, ownership decisions,
+phase transitions, consequential dependency actions, external writes, catalog
+promotion, publication, and acceptance of product scope. This workflow writes
+only exact approved paths and does not approve its own expansion. Model-run
+evaluation, security review, and Standards-plus-Spec review remain with the
+authorized harness or reviewer.
+
+## Output Contract
+
+Return status; selected builder; separate deliverable ids, kinds, and owners;
+target surfaces; runtime visibility and targets; primary and justified support
+paths; operation and identity; catalog result; local content-check results;
+dependency outcomes and provenance; available evaluation and review evidence;
+stable blocker codes; and the lifecycle task that owns the handoff. Never
+describe one product's files as satisfying the other product.
+
+## Completion Gate
+
+Complete construction only when every approved required file exists, bundle
+identities and invocations agree, workflow semantics are substantive, products
+remain separated, support is declared and consumed, protected paths are
+untouched, and local boundary and content checks pass. Overall release also
+requires the task-declared deterministic checks, first-attempt GPT-5.6 Sol
+scenarios, dependency reconciliation, security checks, and Standards-plus-Spec
+review with no critical or high findings; when an outer harness owns those
+gates, report construction completion without claiming release completion.

--- contract ---
# Skill Bundle Contract

Use this contract for every target skill emitted by `$build-workflow-skill`.

## Identity

- Use a lowercase, hyphenated folder.
- Set frontmatter `name` to that exact folder name.
- Put trigger language in frontmatter `description`.
- Include `agents/openai.yaml` with `display_name`, `short_description`, and a
  `default_prompt` that invokes the exact `$skill-name`.
- Make target surface, ownership, runtime visibility, and runtime targets agree
  with the approved deliverable contract.

## Required Semantics

Every substantive `SKILL.md` defines:

1. Overview: the user-visible job and non-goals.
2. Required Context: exact policy, task, source, and prior-art inputs.
3. Input Contract: required and optional inputs plus invalid combinations.
4. Workflow: imperative, independently inspectable steps.
5. Decision Points: branches, selection criteria, and default-deny behavior.
6. Failure Handling: stable blockers, recovery, and stop behavior.
7. Human Boundaries: consequential actions and approvals retained by the user
   or harness.
8. Output Contract: files, state, evidence, and handoff shape.
9. Completion Gate: deterministic, model-run, human-review, and security gates.

Do not replace these semantics with a pointer to another skill or command.

## Optional Resources

Create a reference only for detailed knowledge loaded conditionally. Create a
script only for deterministic repeated work permitted by the repository file
policy. Create an asset only when the workflow directly consumes a stable
template or media resource.

For every resource, record its declared support artifact id, purpose, exact
approval, direct `SKILL.md` reference, consumer deliverable, and verification
evidence. Delete speculative or unreferenced resources.

## Ownership Variants

- Canonical: `.agents/skills/<slug>`; active in the Foundry catalog.
- Project packaged: `projects/<project-slug>/skills/<slug>`; shipped with that
  project and inactive in the Foundry runtime.
- Standalone: product-policy-owned path; inactive in the Foundry catalog and
  evaluated in a disposable repository by default.
- Workflow pack: one complete bundle and independent invocation per member.

An update resolves exactly one existing identity. Promotion is a separate
contract, not an ownership shortcut.

Apply the same strict bundle parser and semantic checks to canonical,
project-packaged, and standalone bundles. Ownership changes path and policy; it
does not permit malformed YAML, duplicate metadata keys, command-first usage,
thin wrappers, undeclared helpers, or missing required semantics.

## Completion Gate

Complete only when:

- required files exist and identities agree;
- the workflow semantics above are substantive;
- optional resources are declared, referenced, and tested;
- command-first and helper-substitute checks pass;
- runtime visibility and catalog membership are correct;
- deterministic fixtures pass;
- required GPT-5.6 Sol fresh-agent scenarios pass on first attempt;
- dependency outcomes reconcile when present;
- Standards and Spec review has no unresolved critical or high finding.

--- behavior ---
# Behavior Evaluation Contract

Use one versioned scenario source for deterministic fixtures and fresh-agent
runs. Store raw prompts, expected contracts, and observed evidence separately;
never reveal grader expectations to the fresh agent.

## Scenario Fields

Each scenario or expanded family variant carries:

- scenario id and family;
- contract version and prompt variant;
- runner modes;
- unaltered raw prompt;
- disposable repository context and policy files;
- expected intent, builder, branch, and deliverable kind;
- ownership boundary, target surface, visibility, and runtime targets;
- required primary bundle and semantics;
- allowed support;
- forbidden outcomes;
- dependency behavior and protected paths;
- starting phase, approval behavior, and expected phase result;
- expected final status, catalog membership, and duplicate count.

## RED And GREEN

1. Keep lifecycle-intent routing separate from implementation-ready
   construction. Lifecycle cases classify or stop; construction cases receive
   the already approved contract but never the hidden expected route.
2. Run a construction prompt without the target builder. Archive the output,
   zero-write proof, and exact rationalization as RED.
3. Run the same prompt with the builder in a clean disposable worktree as
   GREEN.
4. Use GPT-5.6 Sol only. If it is unavailable, record a blocked run; never use a
   fallback model.
5. Give the runner no expected route, hidden classification, forbidden outcome,
   or rubric.
6. Grade actual contract state, emitted files, diff, catalog projection,
   dependency evidence, and phase result.
7. Preserve first-attempt results and isolate every scenario from prior output.

## Evidence Fields

Record:

- evidence id, scenario id, scenario and builder contract versions;
- runner mode, explicit GPT-5.6 Sol identity, Codex CLI version, session id,
  start/completion timestamps, and `first_attempt: true`;
- raw prompt and repository context;
- exact raw runner output plus expected and observed route;
- observed ownership, deliverable contracts, artifacts, visibility, catalog
  membership, phase result, and dependency outcomes;
- full created/updated diff, or explicit verified zero-write evidence for a
  blocked/refused case;
- validation results from an independent deterministic, agent, human, or E2E
  grader with a different identity and session from the runner;
- observed rationalizations;
- pass/fail result, blocker or failure codes, and notes.

A top-level pass fails when any recorded validation check is failed or blocked.
Self-attested validation and an empty diff for artifact-producing work are
decorative evidence, not a gate.

## Failure Codes

Use stable codes:

```text
migration-pending
contract-missing
contract-invalid
ownership-unresolved
phase-approval-unbound
required-artifact-missing
required-artifact-invalid
support-artifact-undeclared
eval-evidence-missing
eval-evidence-failed
dependency-write-plan-missing
dependency-output-missing
dependency-provenance-missing
dependency-role-mismatch
dependency-out-of-boundary
catalog-bundle-incomplete
catalog-phantom-entry
review-evidence-missing
primary-deliverable-incomplete
python-substitute
javascript-only
helper-only
thin-wrapper
command-first
incomplete-metadata
identity-mismatch
decorative-eval
unreferenced-helper
```

## Release Threshold

- Every deterministic fixture passes.
- Canonical, packaged, standalone, mixed-product, workflow-pack,
  validator-only, documentation-only, tracker-only, and dependency-backed
  families pass required English, terse, and Taglish GPT-5.6 Sol runs.
- Ambiguous ownership stops with zero writes.
- Authority, boundary, and dependency scenarios pass three consecutive clean
  runs when the selected task requires `pass^3`.
- A retry after grader feedback never counts as a first-attempt pass.

Use when artifact contracts must be routed to their proper builders and kept within distinct ownership boundaries. +--- + +# Mixed Workflow + +## Overview + +Construct every approved primary artifact in an implementation-ready mixed-product task while preserving the boundary between callable skills and other products. Produce a complete skill bundle only for a skill contract; materialize UI, documentation, validation, or package contracts at their own declared surfaces. Do not approve contracts, alter lifecycle state, publish artifacts, or disguise a non-skill deliverable as a skill. + +## Required Context + +Read the repository policy, the selected implementation task, its approved deliverable migration, phase approvals, artifact bindings, and dependency plan. For each contract, capture its operation, kind, ownership boundary, owner, target surface, runtime visibility, runtime targets, required artifacts, allowed support artifacts, completion conditions, and supplied prior evaluation evidence. Load the exact builder contract for each deliverable kind before writing it. Treat protected paths and existing user changes as immutable unless explicitly approved. + +## Input Contract + +Require exactly one selected task in its approved implementation phase and at least two primary deliverable contracts, including one callable skill and one non-skill product. Every artifact must have a matching deliverable id, artifact id, role, approved phase path, and binding. A create operation requires an unoccupied identity; an update requires exactly one existing identity. Dependencies require a complete, in-boundary write plan and provenance. + +Optional inputs are declared support artifacts, dependency outputs, and harness-supplied RED evidence. Reject pending migrations, unresolved ownership, unbound paths, duplicate identities, conflicting create/update operations, support files without approval, or a path assigned to more than one ownership boundary. Never infer approval from a linked-artifact list alone. + +## Workflow + +1. Parse the selected task and enumerate every primary contract and exact artifact binding. +2. Validate migration, phase, ownership, operation, identity, runtime, dependency, and approval fields before any write. +3. Partition contracts by deliverable kind. Route only callable skill contracts through the skill-bundle builder; route each other contract through its own product boundary. +4. For every skill, create a substantive `SKILL.md` and matching `agents/openai.yaml` at the exact approved target. Keep its name, invocation, ownership, and visibility consistent. +5. For every non-skill product, create a minimally complete but substantive native artifact at its target. Do not place skill metadata inside that surface. +6. Add support files only when their artifact ids and paths are explicitly allowed and a primary artifact consumes them. +7. Inspect the resulting tree and contents. Check required files, identities, invocations, semantics, cross-boundary leakage, undeclared resources, and protected paths. +8. Run only the deterministic local checks authorized to this construction step. Preserve harness-supplied evidence without editing it. +9. Report each deliverable, boundary, surface, visibility, artifact path, check result, and stable blocker. Leave lifecycle transitions and release gates to the invoking harness. + +## Decision Points + +| Condition | Action | +| --- | --- | +| Contract kind is a callable skill | Build a complete skill bundle at its declared skill surface. | +| Contract kind is UI, package, document, or validator | Use its native builder and keep it outside the skill bundle. | +| Operation is `create` and target is free | Create only approved primary paths. | +| Operation is `update` with one identity | Modify only that resolved identity. | +| Ownership, identity, approval, or dependency plan is unclear | Default deny; stop before writes. | +| One deliverable fails after another succeeds | Preserve valid in-boundary work, report partial status, and identify the failed contract. | + +Canonical skills are runtime-active at `.agents/skills//`. Project-packaged products remain project-local unless their own contract says otherwise. Never promote, publish, or add catalog entries by inference. + +## Failure Handling + +Stop before writing for `migration-pending`, `contract-missing`, `contract-invalid`, `ownership-unresolved`, `phase-approval-unbound`, or `dependency-write-plan-missing`. After construction, use `required-artifact-missing`, `required-artifact-invalid`, `support-artifact-undeclared`, `identity-mismatch`, `primary-deliverable-incomplete`, or `dependency-out-of-boundary` as applicable. Do not weaken a contract, create a substitute helper, or turn a UI directory into a skill bundle to obtain a pass. Report observed paths, the affected deliverable, and the owner or approval needed for recovery. + +## Human Boundaries + +The user or outer lifecycle harness retains approval of contracts, ownership, phase paths, dependency side effects, promotion, publishing, and consequential external actions. This workflow may write only approved paths within declared product boundaries. It must not launch nested agents, model evaluations, reviews, lifecycle calls, network operations, or external writes when those gates belong to the harness. + +## Output Contract + +Return a structured handoff containing status; selected builder; deliverable kinds; ownership boundaries; target surfaces; runtime visibility; created or updated artifact paths; stable blocker codes; and a concise validation summary. The file tree is the construction evidence. Distinguish completed, partial, and blocked outcomes, and never claim model-run or human-review evidence that the current execution did not perform. + +## Completion Gate + +Construction is complete only when every approved primary artifact exists at its bound path; each skill has matching identity metadata and all required workflow semantics; every non-skill deliverable is substantive and native to its surface; no undeclared support or cross-boundary files exist; and authorized local content checks pass.
